Out of position with a weak kicker, my Q♥6♠ is highly vulnerable to better Qx, two‑pair/sets and to diamond turn cards; Elon’s sizing looks value‑heavy (plus some semi‑bluffs) and I don’t want to inflate the pot OOP with a marginal top pair. A reraise would overcommit with limited playability. I fold and preserve my stack for a better, positionally favourable spot.

Heads-up with A7o on the button (SB) is a hand I want to play aggressively — it's strong in position and has good showdown value with an ace. Limping/calling 50 surrenders initiative and lets the big blind see flops cheaply; folding is too weak. I'll open to 3x the BB (300 total) to take the initiative, deny correct pot odds to many of his marginal hands, and build a pot I can play postflop in position. Size: 300 total (I’ve posted 50, so I’d add 250). If Elon 3-bets big I’ll re-evaluate (often folding or defending based on sizing and SPR); vs a single call I plan to C-bet favorable boards and use position to steer the hand.

Heads-up play with deep stacks (220BB effective). In BB facing a button/SB min-raise to 300 (3BB). 84s is a speculative suited connector with straight and flush potential. OOP but heads-up dynamics favor calling wide to realize equity against a wide button opening range. Pot odds excellent: calling 200 into 400 pot (3:1 direct, plus implied). Folding too tight preflop; 3-betting risks bloating pot OOP with marginal hand. Plan to play postflop cautiously, looking to float or hit draws.

Heads-up on a draw-heavy flop (5h-6h-Td) with two hearts and connected cards favoring many straight/flush draws in villain's button range. 84cc gives a gutshot straight draw (4 outs to 7 for 4-5-6-7-8), backdoor club flush draw, and overcard with 8, but overall weak equity (~20% vs wide range). OOP after flat preflop, pot control is key with deep effective stacks (~220BB). Donk betting risks folding out better and getting raised off equity; better to check, induce bluffs/value from strong hands, call small bets to realize equity/implied odds, fold to large bets. Preserves stack for better spots over 150 hands.

I opened preflop with A7o and took the initiative. Flop 5h Td 6h — villain checked. Pot is 600 and stacks are deep (SPR ≈ 28), so I don’t want to build a large pot with just Ace-high, but I do want to deny equity to heart draws and fold out many Kx/Qx/Jx and weak holdings from his calling range. A small continuation bet (~1/3 pot) maximizes fold equity while keeping the pot controllable if called. Plan: bet 200 to take down the pot often; if he calls I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn (check hearts or a Ten, consider a second small barrel on safe cards); if he check-raises big I’ll fold this marginal hand.

Heads-up OOP on a draw-heavy flop (5h-6h-Td) with 84cc, holding a gutshot straight draw (4 outs to the 7 for 4-5-6-7-8) plus backdoor club flush draw and live 8 for overcard potential. Equity ~25-30% vs Minni's wide SB/Button donk range (which includes many draws, Ax, Tx, pocket pairs, bluffs). Her small donk bet (200 into 600, ~1/3 pot) screams weak protection or thin value, not screaming strength. Excellent pot odds (200 into 800 for 4:1 direct + massive implied with 220BB effective stacks and straight/flush redraw potential). Folding squanders equity in tournament with 71 hands left; raising bloats pot OOP with marginal hand and risks villain folding better or jamming worse. Calling keeps pot small, realizes equity cheaply, preserves stack for better spots while inducing bluffs on later streets. Plan: Call, check turn if miss (pot control), barrel if improve or as semi-bluff on scary turns.

Heads-up OOP on turn with 84cc facing deep stacks (~170-220BB effective). Board extremely draw-heavy: 5h-6h-Td-Jh completes nut flush draws (any two hearts like AhKh, QhJh, etc.), adds straight draws (78, 47, 89), and pairs J/T. My gutshot straight draw to 7 (4 outs ~8%) remains, but no flush draw progress (backdoor clubs only), 8 high overcard has little showdown value. Equity ~15-25% vs Minni's polarized SB donk range (weak Tx/Ax/pairs/draws that called my check-call, bluffs). Her small flop donk (200 into 600) suggests protection/thin value/draw, not monsters. Pot 1000; betting risks raising off equity OOP with marginal hand, folding out her bluffs/worse draws, bloating pot. Checking induces bluffs from missed draws (she likely has hearts), controls pot size, realizes equity cheaply if she bets small (great odds), allows folding to big bets without commitment. With 71 hands left in tournament, stack preservation key over speculative shoving. Plan: Check, call small bets/floats with odds, fold to large overbets; value if miraculously improve to straight on river.

I opened A7o and C-bet the flop to deny equity; Elon called. The turn J♥ brings a third heart to the board (5♥ T♦ 6♥ J♥). I have only Ace-high with no heart blocker, while his calling range contains many hands that now have significant improvement (two-heart combos that just made a flush, Jx, Tx, or other pairs). With the pot ~1,000 and deep stacks, barreling again is too risky — I lack value and would be forced to fold to serious aggression. Checking controls the pot, lets me realize showdown if he checks back or call a small bet, and keeps the option to fold to a sizable turn shove. Action: check and evaluate river.

River brings the Th, pairing the Td for a board of 5h-6h-Td-Jh-Th. This heavily favors flushes (nut flush with any two hearts, many combos in Minni's SB donk range like Ahxh, Khxh, Qhxh), two-pair Tx (T9s, T7s, etc.), full houses unlikely, straights with 78, and trips T. My 84cc completely missed: gutshot to 7 gone (~0 straight outs now), no flush, no pair, pure 8-high bluff catcher at best. Equity dismal (~10-20% vs her polarized range of value [Tx/sets/flushes] and bluffs [missed draws/A-high]). Preflop SB minraise + small flop donk (protection/draw) + turn check (weak or trapping) suggests she doesn't have the nuts often, but river improves many of her draws. OOP first to act in 1000 pot with 170BB+ effective stacks and 71 hands remaining; betting/raising commits too much to a bluff against a calling station-ish opponent (from hand history: Minni calls down light, e.g., Hand 7,9,33) and risks larger pot OOP. Checking maximizes EV: induces bluffs from missed hearts/A-high/stone bluffs, gets showdown value with 8-high vs her weak check-backs, controls pot size, preserves stack for superior spots. Fold to any reasonable bet; no regret as we got to river cheaply with live equity earlier. Tournament survival > variance here.

I opened pre with A7s and c-bet the flop; villain called. The turn J♥ completed many heart draws and I checked to control the pot — that was correct. The river T♥ pairs the board and now there are four hearts on board (5♥ T♦ 6♥ J♥ T♥). I have no heart and only Ace-high; many hands that called the flop (any heart, Jx, Tx, pocket pairs) now beat me. Betting here would be a thin bluff with very poor fold equity (I don’t block hearts) and would get called by all completed flushes and most pairs. With essentially no value and high likelihood of being called or crushed, the correct play is to check and take showdown (and fold to a river shove). Action: check.

Heads-up on the button/SB with deep stacks (216bb effective). Qh6h is a strong suited queen with good playability, straight/flush potential. In heads-up, SB raises a wide range pre-flop to steal blinds or build pots with position advantage overall. Minni defends wide based on history, but raising takes initiative OOP post-flop. Standard raise to 3x BB (300 total). Calling is too passive; folding too tight. This maximizes EV by denying equity and setting up post-flop aggression.

Heads-up, I'm in the big blind with T9♥ — a strong, playable hand against a wide button opening range. Elon opened to 300 from the button (pot 400, I must call 200). Against his frequent button opens I want to both get value and generate fold equity rather than just defending. A 3-bet to 900 (≈3x his open) is a standard size here: it pressures weaker broadways, small pairs and some suited connectors, narrows his continuing range, and still leaves plenty of stack to play postflop if called. If he 4-bets large I'll re-evaluate (fold or call based on size); if he calls I'll proceed with a constructive c-bet plan on favorable flops and use the hand's good playability. Raising to 500 (minimum) is too small to achieve those goals, and simply calling gives him too cheap a flop. Therefore I raise to 900.

Heads-up deep-stacked (200+ BB effective) on the button/SB facing a 3-bet to 900 after my open to 300. Pot 1200, calling 600 more (~4:1 odds). Qh6h is a strong suited queen with straight/flush potential and good playability heads-up. Minni's BB 3-bet range is likely wide (per history: aggressive, frequent raises/3-bets OOP), including Ax, broadway, suited hands, bluffs. Q6s has ~38-42% equity vs a loose 3-bet range (15-25% hands), solid vs tighter. Folding is too tight EV-wise long-term; 4-betting OOP risks bloating pot with marginal hand if called, and polarization doesn't fit well (preflop initiative already contested). Calling realizes equity, keeps pot multiway potential postflop, sets up check-raises or floats. OOP postflop but deep stacks allow flexible play; history shows Minni donk bets often, exploitable. Maximizes EV over 70 remaining hands by denying folds and building stack gradually.

Flop: 8♥ A♥ 9♦. I hold T♥9♥ — a pair of nines with a strong heart backdoor (4 hearts out there, one heart on the board is the Ace). Preflop I 3-bet to 900 and he called, so his continuing range will include a lot of Ax (which currently beat me), broadways and some connectors and pockets that have missed. This is a good spot to continuation-bet: I have decent showdown value vs missed broadways and I can charge/deny equity to heart draws and overcards. I’ll make a half‑pot c‑bet (~50%) — big enough to fold out many unpaired hands and to protect my pair, but small enough relative to stacks to keep maneuverability if he raises. Plan: if he folds I take the pot; if he calls I re-evaluate on the turn (and proceed cautiously vs aggression from an Ace-heavy range).

Heads-up OOP on a draw-heavy flop Ah8h9d with Qh6h giving me a strong Queen-high flush draw (9 outs, ~35% equity standalone). Pot 2700, facing half-pot donk bet 900 from aggressive Minni who frequently donk bets wide ranges including Ax, pairs, draws, and bluffs per history. Calling realizes equity but OOP risks tough turns/rivers; folding too tight with great implied odds and 200bb deep stacks. Raising semi-bluffs profitably: applies pressure on worse Ax/draws/bluffs, gets value from weaker made hands, takes initiative back. Sizing to ~3x bet (2700 total) for pot-committal pressure without overcommitting. Maximizes EV by folding out equity and extracting when ahead, building stack in tournament.

Heads‑up after my 3‑bet I c‑bet the flop (A♥ 8♥ 9♦) with T♥9♥ and Elon 3‑bet my flop bet to 2,700. I currently have second pair (9s) plus a very strong heart draw (4 hearts total), so I have lots of equity: roughly 9 hearts + remaining tens/nines = ~14 raw outs (≈50% to improve by the river), and even a turn heart alone gives me a lot of fold equity and often the best hand. Pot odds: I must call 1,800 more into a 5,400 pot (need ~25% equity). Versus Elon's calling/raising range after a preflop call (many A‑x, broadways, some pairs and occasional bluffs) my hand’s equity is well above the breakeven threshold. Folding would surrender too much equity; reraising out of position with only second pair + draw is thin and needlessly bloats the pot. I’ll call the 1,800 (match the 2,700 total on the flop), see the turn, and proceed based on the card: press for value if I hit a heart/T/9 or fold to heavy turn aggression if the board pairs an Ace or becomes very unfavorable.

Board: 8♥ A♥ 9♦ 3♦. I hold T♥9♥ — second pair (nines) plus the nut heart draw. Pot = 7,200, it’s checked to me on the turn. I have 9 heart outs + 3 remaining tens + 2 remaining nines = 14 unique outs (~14/46 ≈ 30.4% to improve on the river). Elon’s flop raise showed aggression, but his turn check looks like pot‑control or hesitation — a good spot to apply pressure with a semi‑bluff. Betting puts pressure on unpaired hands and non‑nut draws, builds the pot when I hit, and gives me fold equity I won’t get by checking. I’ll bet about half the pot (3,600). If he folds I scoop 7,200; if he calls I have strong equity to improve on the river; if he raises big I’ll re‑evaluate (likely fold to a polarized shove unless I can credibly put him on bluffs).

Heads-up OOP on turn board Ah8h9d3d with Qh6h: queen-high flush draw (9 outs ~19% equity), overcards, gutshot potential (to 7 for BDFD/straight), solid playability deep (~160bb effective pre-turn). Pot 10800, facing Minni's 1/3-pot donk-lead 3600 after calling my strong flop raise (repping top Ax, sets, combos). Her range: polarized strong (AhX, sets 88/99/A9, two-pair), strong draws (KhJh/Jh10h/10h7h flush draws, diamond draws like KdQd/Jd10d), fewer bluffs given history of aggression but folding to river bets when behind. Equity ~30-35% vs her calling/leading range (blocks some Qh, playability crushes worse draws). Folding negative EV (great implied odds, 3:1 direct + stacks); raising risks vs nutted hands (min 7200 commits ~40% stack, her 11070 can shove), low fold equity post her call. Calling realizes equity, keeps pot controlled, position to bluff/maneuver river if miss or value bet if hit. History shows Minni value-calls light but folds draws to aggression later; pot control optimal for stack preservation/growth over 70 hands.

Board: 8♥ A♥ 9♦ 3♦ 8♦. My hand: T♥9♥ → two pair, 9s and 8s (A kicker). Opponent: opened on the button, called my 3‑bet, check‑raised the flop big, then check‑called my turn bet and now checked the river. That line is very consistent with a heavy Ace‑range (and a subset of sets/pairs) — many of which beat my two pair. Betting the river would fold out some pure air but will also get called by a lot of Ax hands that currently beat me, so a thin value bet is likely negative EV and a larger bet just invites a raise from the few better hands. With showdown value and a high chance Elon is holding an Ace or a slow‑played strong hand, the lowest‑variance, +EV play is to check back and realize my equity at showdown rather than risk getting raised or giving value to better hands. I’ll check and go to showdown.

River board 8h Ah 9d 3d 8d pairs the 8s, bringing a diamond flush possibility and trips. Heads-up OOP (SB first postflop but her check gives me last action effectively). Pot 14400, effective stacks ~11k (hers). Qh6h is queen-high with heart blockers (Qh blocks broadway hearts, 6h lower draws); no made hand but blocks some flush/straight draws and overcards. Her range: 3bet pre (Ax, broadway, suited connectors, draws), flop donk-call raise (strong Ax like AhK/AQ/AT, sets 88/99, two-pair A9/89, strong draws KhJh/10h7h hearts, diamond draws KdQd), turn donk-call (continues value with top/mid pairs/trips/draws improving or semi-bluffing). River check: polarized - nuts (diamond flushes KdQd+, Ah8h trips, 88 set) trapping/inducing; medium strength (Ax no pair, missed draws now high card/bluff catchers) pot control; unlikely pure air after two leads. My equity ~20-30% vs her range (beats missed draws J10h/10h7h <Q, some Ax no kicker; loses to all made hands/pairs). SDV low but present vs bluffs/draws. Check-behind: realize equity but likely lose at SD (~30% winrate); pot control safe for survival but misses value/fold equity. Bet bluff: high fold equity vs her medium hands/draws (history shows folds to river bets when checked after aggression, e.g., Hand 64/51); thin value from worse Ax/draws; blocks strong combos. Sizing 7200 (~1/2 pot) balances pressure (pot odds 3:1 for her call), commits her stack (~65% left after), low SPR post-call for her hero-fold tough. Max EV long-term: denies her equity, builds stack aggressively with 70 hands left/deep tournament.

I have two pair (9s & 8s) on 8♥ A♥ 9♦ 3♦ 8♦. Elon’s line (call of my 3‑bet preflop, big check‑raise on the flop, check‑call on the turn, now a river bet) is weighted toward strong value (A9/A8, 99/88, sets) but also contains missed heart draws and weaker A‑x that I beat. The pot is 21,600 after his 7,200 bet; calling 7,200 would make the final pot 28,800, so I need ≈25% equity to justify a call. My two‑pair beats most missed draws and many Ax bluffs and has enough showdown equity versus his polarized range to clear that threshold. Raising all‑in would only polarize the pot further and get called by better hands; folding surrenders a price that’s too good. I’ll call 7,200 to get to showdown (leaving ~3,870 behind).

Heads-up on the button with A♥T♠ is a clear open-raise hand: strong high-card value and plays very well post-flop in position. The pot is 150 and it’s only 50 to call, so I’ll take the initiative and raise to a standard 3x BB (300 total) to gain fold equity and isolate Elon’s wide BB defending range. This sizing extracts value and keeps the pot manageable against his 72BB stack — I can comfortably call a small/medium 3-bet to realize equity from position, but will re-evaluate (and potentially fold) to very large 3-bets. If called I plan to c-bet favorable runouts and use positional advantage to control the hand.

Heads-up against Minni on the button who raised to 300 (3x BB). My hand 7c4d is offsuit trash with minimal playability, no straight potential against a strong range, and poor post-flop potential out of position. Pot odds are 400:200 (2:1), but her position, larger stack (32k vs my 7k), and history of frequent aggression make defending unprofitable. Standard fold to preserve stack and wait for better spots in HU play.

Heads-up on the button/SB with 9h4c offsuit, a very weak hand with poor playability, low equity against any defense, and bad post-flop prospects. Position is good but hand too weak for a raise or limp. Standard play is to fold trash hands like this from SB to avoid difficult spots, preserving stack for better opportunities. Minni has a massive stack and defends wide, but no value in playing this.

Heads-up on the button with Qh8h — a playable, suited hand with good blockers and postflop playability. Pot is 150 and I only need to call 50; min-raise is to 200. Elon has shown an aggressive tendency both preflop and postflop but has a much shorter stack (7,080) than I do, so I want to both seize the initiative and keep pot size manageable. I raise to 300 (3x BB) to apply pressure, pick up the blind when he folds, and represent a stronger range; the size is big enough to force folds from many marginal hands but small enough that I won't overcommit with Q8s. Postflop plan: c-bet favorable/dry textures, float or play position on draw-heavy boards, and fold to large 3-bets or shoves (or re-evaluate vs a small 3-bet), avoiding bloating the pot without a premium hand.

Heads-up vs Minni on the button/SB who raised to 300 (standard sizing). My hand 2h3h is a weak suited connector, low cards with limited playability OOP. While pot odds are favorable (200 to call into 400+), equity realization postflop is poor due to position and hand strength—hard to connect meaningfully without being dominated. Minni is aggressive but deep-stacked (325bb vs my 70bb); folding preserves my stack for premium spots to maximize tournament EV. Defending this wide is -EV long-term in BB.

Heads-up pre-flop on the button/SB with 3d5d, suited connectors with good playability for straights and flushes. Position is excellent, pot odds are great (50 to call into 150 pot, ~3.5:1). Deep stacks provide implied odds for hitting draws. Folding is far too tight in heads-up button vs BB. Raising could work but limping allows seeing a cheap flop in position while keeping pot small. Opponent has been aggressive historically, but we'll have position post-flop.

Heads-up in the big blind with Tc Kd. Elon (the button/small blind) just limp-called, which represents a wide, often weak range. K‑T offsuit is a strong heads‑up hand and too good to just check behind every time. I have a large stack advantage (32820 vs 6980), so I should apply pressure and isolate the limp to either take the pot now or play a reasonably-sized pot with the initiative. Raising to 400 total (I’ve already posted 100, so I add 300) is a clean, standard sized raise vs a limp: big enough to get folds from many weak hands and charge speculative holdings, but not oversized to overcommit. Plan: c‑bet favorable/dry flops, proceed cautiously on coordinated boards, and fold or re-evaluate if Elon shows strong resistance (large 3‑bet).

Heads-up on the button/SB with 3d5d suited connectors. Excellent post-flop position and playability for straights/flushes. Facing Minni's raise to 400 total (300 to call) into 500 pot, offering pot odds of roughly 800:300 or 2.67:1, requiring ~27% equity which this hand has against her likely wide BB raising range after my limp. Minni is aggressive pre-flop historically, but I crush many of her calling/raising hands post-flop. Calling realizes implied odds with deep effective stacks (~70BB), allows cheap flop in position. 3-betting risks bloating pot with speculative hand; folding too tight heads-up. Plan to play draws aggressively if hit, fold if miss badly.

Heads‑up after I raised pre and Elon just called. I hold K♦ T♣ on a dry Q♦ 4♦ 5♣ flop — no pair but a king over the queen and a backdoor diamond/runner‑runner flush possibility. Elon’s limp‑call pre indicates a very wide, often weak range (lots of misses here). As the pre‑flop raiser I should continue to apply pressure and deny equity to his wide calling range. I’ll make a standard c‑bet of about half the pot (400 into 800) — big enough to fold many weak hands and charge draws, but small enough to keep the pot controllable against his short stack. If he calls I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn and re‑evaluate if he raises big or jams (I’ll fold to a large, polarizing raise unless I pick up equity or have a strong read).

Heads-up on the button facing Minni's flop c-bet of 400 into 1200 pot (~3:1 odds, needing ~25% equity). Holding 3d5d on 4d Qd 5c flop: bottom pair (5s), nut flush draw (4 diamonds), gutshot straight draw (to 2 or 6). Excellent equity (~40-50% vs her likely polarized BB range after raising limper: Ax, broadways, sets, two-pair, draws). Position allows controlling pot size post-flop. Deep effective stacks (67BB) provide massive implied odds if we hit flush/straight/set. Raising risks overplaying current strength or facing shove from better; folding absurd with this hand. Call realizes equity cheaply, disguises strength, sets up turns/rivers to aggress or value bet.

Check. I raised pre and c-bet the flop and Elon called, but the turn (5♠) pairs the board and drastically reduces my equity. I have K♦T♣ with no pair and effectively only three clean river outs (three kings) versus a likely range that now includes Qx, pocket 5s/trips, and other pairs. A river improvement chance of ~6% (and no realistic flush possibility) means I have almost no value-betting equity. Betting here would be a pure bluff with limited fold equity against a short-stacked opponent who can simply shove or call; if he shoved I’d have to fold and risk losing a bigger chunk than necessary. Pot-control is the correct play: check to keep the pot small, see what Elon does on the river, and only continue with a clear plan (call small, fold to large bets, or bluff selectively on favorable rivers).

Turn board pairs the 5c with 5s, giving me trips (set of 5s) with 3d5d on 4d Qd 5c 5s. Monster hand: disguised strength from limp-call line, nut diamond flush draw still live (four diamonds to any diamond), open-ended straight draw potential (3-4-5 to 2-6 or 6 makes straight with trips). Board vulnerable to Qx, Ax draws, flush draws, two-pair combos that beat bluffs but we crush. Minni's flop c-bet then turn check suggests polarized range: weak (missed draw, air) checking behind, or trap with strong hand, but our disguised line caps us as weak/draw. Deep effective stacks (my 6280 ~40BB, pot 1600 SPR ~4:1), but shallower side allows aggressive value extraction. Betting extracts from Q-high, top pair, busted draws, two-pair while protecting vs unlikely better hands (quads, full house rare). Sizing 1100 (~2/3 pot) balances value/protection, denies equity to draws (river diamond or 6 hurts minimally). Checking risks giving free card to draws or missing value from calling stations. Position allows seeing river if raised.

Heads‑up, I checked the turn to control the pot after c‑betting the flop. Elon’s 1,100 into a 2,700 pot is a relatively small probe from a short stack; it can be value (Qx, a 5, small pair) but also a wide bluff. With K♦T♣ I have no pair and only a few clean river outs (mainly kings), so I don’t want to bloat the pot by raising and risk getting shoved off my hand or calling a large jam. Pot‑odds for a call are ~29% (1,100 to win 3,800), and calling keeps the pot manageable while allowing me to realise any equity and see the river. Plan: call the 1,100 to see the river; fold to a large shove if Elon jams all‑in, otherwise re‑evaluate on the river for a possible bluff or fold depending on the card and action.

I have K♦T♣ on Q♦-4♦-5♣-5♠-K♥ — I turned an overcard into top pair on the river. Elon limp‑called pre, called my flop c‑bet, then bet the turn when I checked: that line is consistent with value hands (Qx, 5x/trips, small pairs) and a few semi‑bluffs (diamond combos). Pot is 3,800 and Elon has ~5,180 behind. Betting river for thin value risks getting check‑raised or shoved off by hands that already crush me (pocket 5s / full houses) and also denies the opportunity to induce bluffs from missed draws. Checking keeps the pot under control, preserves my stack advantage, and lets Elon make the next move — I can then call reasonable river probes from bluffs/weak pairs and fold to a polarized shove. Plan: check. If Elon bets small (roughly <= ~2k) I will call for value; if he jams (~5k) I will fold to avoid calling into a range that realistically contains many full houses/trips.

River board completes with Kh, no flush or straight obvious besides possible gutshots. Holding 3d5d for trips (set of 5s: 5d/5c/5s), extremely strong disguised hand from limp-call-flop call-turn bet line, which caps our range low. Nut diamond flush draw and straight draws were live pre-river but missed; still crush her likely range: Qx (TPTK/TPS), Ax, lower pairs, busted diamond draws (huge from flop), KQ/Kd post-river. Her turn call after check then river check-back suggests medium strength wary of aggression or weakness checking through. Deep her stacks (31020), my effective 5180 limits max bet but pot 3800 SPR ~1.3:1 post-bet. Value bet extracts max from worse (Qx calls ~60-70% pot), denies free showdown, protects vs unlikely villain (K5s, Q5s rare). 2600 (~2/3 pot) balances value/protection, leaves room if raised (can shove over jams), fits villain calling station tendencies from history (frequent calls with marginal hands). Check risks free card to draws or missing value from passive lines.

I turned top pair (K♦T♣ on Q♦‑4♦‑5♣‑5♠‑K♥). Elon’s line (limp pre, call flop, bet turn after my check, big bet river) can be value-heavy but also includes many missed diamond draws and medium pairs/Qx that I beat. The only realistic hands that crush me are pocket 5s (very few combos) or rare KK/premium Kx combinations; most of his range (Qx, A‑x, missed draws) is behind. Pot is ~6,400 and I must call 2,600, so break‑even equity ≈29% — my hand has substantially more equity vs his wide line. Raising would be thin value (min‑raise to 5,180), likely price out worse hands and create awkward spots; folding is too passive. I’ll call 2,600 to realize equity and see showdown.

I have 3♣2♥ — a very weak, unsuited hand. I’ve already posted the 50¢ SB and the cost to see a flop is only 50 more, so I’ll take a cheap flop in position rather than open-raise with a hand that’s often dominated. If Elon 3-bets preflop I’ll fold; if we see a favorable flop (pair, backdoor draws) I can proceed, otherwise I’ll keep the pot small and check/fold as needed. Preserving my big stack and using position is the prudent option here.

Preflop in the big blind facing a small blind limp from Minni. My hand is 5h3d, which is a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability and low equity. However, since it's heads-up effectively and I've already invested the blind, getting a free flop multiway (but just vs SB) is +EV. No need to raise a trash hand into her wide limping range, as she'd likely call or 3-bet light, leaving me in tough spots postflop. Checking allows me to realize equity when I hit (e.g., two-pair, straight draws) or fold to aggression cheaply. Deep stacks favor playing postflop. Minni limps wide from SB, so board will hit her range often, but checking is disciplined.

Flop board 5s Ah 4c gives me middle pair (fives) with the 5h topping my 3d kicker and a gutshot straight draw (needing a 2 or 6). Decent equity overall (~30-40% vs her wide SB limp-call range, which includes many Ax, broadways, suited connectors, pairs below aces, and bluffs). Pot is 200, deep stacks (114 BB effective). As first to act in position postflop (BB vs SB), leading risks folding out better but drawing hands or getting raised off equity. Checking realizes equity, induces bluffs/bets from her Ax/draws, allows check-raise thin value or fold to strong bets. Her history shows she can check weak limped hands or bet aggressively with hits; pot control preferred with marginal strength on A-high board where she hits top pair often. No fold equity needed; checking balances my wide checking range.

I have 3♣2♥ on A♥-5♠-4♣ — that’s A-2-3-4-5: the wheel (the nut straight) on the flop. Elon checked, which can be air, A‑x, or a small/medium pair. I should bet for value and to deny free cards that could make a higher straight (a 6 completing a 2-3-4-5-6 would beat me). A half-pot bet is the right balance: it extracts from Ax and single pairs, induces calls from worse hands, and keeps the pot manageable if Elon check‑raises. Plan: bet 100; if he raises I’ll call and reassess on the turn (I currently have a very strong made hand).

Facing a small 100 bet into 300 pot on an A-high flop where I have middle pair (5s) with a gutshot straight draw (to 2 or 6) and a marginal kicker. Pot odds are excellent (~3:1 direct, better implied). My equity is around 35-45% against Minni's wide SB limping/betting range, which includes Ax (often weak), draws, pocket pairs, and bluffs. Calling keeps the pot small OOP, realizes equity cheaply, induces bluffs from missed draws or weak Ax on turn/river, and allows check-fold/raise on scary turns. Raising now risks bloating the pot with a marginal hand vulnerable to Ax raises or calls, and her small sizing suggests not super strength. Folding is -EV given price and playability. Deep stacks favor calling to see turn developments (possible straight or pair improvement). Consistent with prior check to pot control on dangerous board.

On the turn, the board is 5s Ah 4c Th, giving me middle pair (5s) with a weak 3d kicker and a lingering gutshot straight draw (to 2 or 6). The Th is somewhat of a blank for my hand but brings straight draw possibilities (e.g., JQ) and adds a second heart, improving some flush draws and broadway hands in Minni's range. Pot is 400, and I act first OOP with deep effective stacks (mine ~113 BB, hers deeper). Her small flop bet (100 into 300) after limping SB suggests a wide range: weak Ax, mid pairs, draws (straight/flush potential), floats. Equity remains decent (~30-40% vs her range) due to pair + gutshot, with implied odds if I improve to two pair/straight. Betting risks raising off equity from better Ax or getting called by draws/pairs I dominate minimally; thin value bets OOP are tough to realize. Checking induces bets from her missed draws/weak Ax, allows bluff-catching or check-raise thin value on strong rivers, and controls pot size on dangerous board where she can have top pair often. Folding impossible as first to act. Consistent with flop/turn pot control strategy against aggressive SB opponent who value bets thin but folds to check-raises. Deep stacks favor seeing river cheaply.

I have the nut straight (A-2-3-4-5). Elon called a small flop bet and then checked the turn — his range is likely Ax or single pairs that will call for value. Only a 6 on the river can make a higher straight (4 outs), so I should bet for value and to charge those outs. A sizable but not overcommittal bet (about 3/4 pot) will extract calls from worse hands (A-x, pairs) while denying proper odds to draw-heavy hands and keeping the pot manageable with deep stacks. If he calls I’ll re-evaluate the river and continue extracting; if he check-raises very large it likely represents a 6 or a rare monster and I’ll re-assess (leaning to call medium raises given how few hands beat me).

Facing a 300 bet into 400 pot on the turn (pot odds ~3.3:1 effective, needing ~23% equity) with middle pair (5h) + gutshot straight draw (to 2x or 6x for wheel/straight) and backdoor heart potential. Board: 5s Ah 4c Th is draw-heavy (hearts flush draw, straight draws like 2-3, 6-7, JQ), favoring Minni's aggressive SB limp/bet range which includes weak Ax (A2-A9o/s), mid pairs (66-TT, vulnerable to my pair), draws (56s, 23s, hearts), floats, and bluffs. My equity ~35-45% vs her range (pair dominates worse pairs/draws, gutshot adds outs). Folding -EV given price and playability; raising OOP bloats pot with marginal hand vulnerable to Ax shove/5-bet or draws realizing; thin value raise risks getting called by better. Calling realizes equity cheaply, induces river bluffs from missed draws/weak Ax, controls pot OOP on scary board, sets up check-fold/raise/value bet on bricks/improvers. Deep stacks (~113BB mine) favor seeing river; consistent with pot control strategy vs Minni's thin value/aggression shown in prior hands (e.g., small bets with mid strength, calls big pots but folds to pressure). Her turn sizing small again suggests not nuts.

River pairs the 4c with 4d, significantly improving my hand from middle pair of 5s + gutshot to two pair (5s and 4s using board's paired 4s and my 5h pairing board 5s). This crushes Minni's likely SB limping/betting range: weak Ax (top pair), unpaired broadways, smaller pairs, busted heart/straight draws (e.g., 6-7, J-Q, backdoor flushes). Vulnerable mainly to unlikely sets (55 for boat, 44 quads), 4x trips (possible but limp-bet sizing thin), or wheel straight (A-5-4-3-2, she might have 2x or 6-3 blocking my gutshot outs). Her small flop (100 into ~300) and turn (300 into 500) bets indicate polarized range: thin value (weak Ax, small pairs) or draws rather than nuts, consistent with her history of small aggressive bets post-limp with marginal strength but calling larger amounts OOP. Pot 1000, deep stacks (110BB effective), OOP first to act: betting extracts value from worse hands that call (Ax especially stubborn), protects against river bluffs she might check behind weak, and denies her equity if she has draw. Sizing 60% pot balances value/protection without overbetting into monsters. Checking realizes equity but risks free showdown or facing bet I call anyway, missing value from Ax/draws; pot control no longer needed post-improvement. Consistent with evolving from pot control (marginal flop/turn) to value betting strength.

I have the nut straight (A-2-3-4-5) with 3♣2♥ on 5♠ A♥ 4♣ T♥ 4♦. Very few hands beat me — only full houses/quads (pocket 4s, pocket 5s, AA, combos like A4 or 4+5) — and ties are only possible if Elon holds both a 2 and a 3. Elon’s line (checked preflop in the BB, called my small flop bet, called my larger turn bet, then a modest raise on the river) looks like a calling/medium-strength range (Ax, one-pair, some medium pockets), not an obviously locked full-house line. Calling 600 into the ~1600 pot gives excellent pot odds and keeps worse hands paying off; a raise to 1200 would risk folding out many thin callers and would unnecessarily inflate the pot against the small chance he has a full house/quads. I’ll call the 600 and extract value while retaining the option to fold to a large shove if he shows extreme strength.

Heads-up preflop from SB vs BB. 68dd is a premium suited connector heads-up, with excellent playability, straight/flush potential, and domination potential postflop. Effective button position postflop. Minni limped BB (no raise), so raising to steal blinds or build pot when ahead in equity. Standard sizing ~3x BB at 300. My stack 10k allows aggression without overcommitting. History shows Minni defends wide, but this hand plays well against her calling range.

Elon opened to 300 from the button. The pot is 400 and I need to put in 200 more (300 total including my 100 BB). 5♦7♠ is weak but heads‑up the opener's range is wide and the price is good — calling 200 to play a multi‑street pot gives ~3:1 pot odds (need ~25% equity). I don't want to 3‑bet with this offsuit connector (OOP against a 10k stack) and folding surrenders too much value. Plan: call and see a flop; play cautiously OOP — fold to heavy aggression on dry boards, continue with pairs/draws or use position and pot control to extract value if I improve.

Flop: A♠ 5♠ T♦ — my hand 5♦7♠ = pair of fives with a weak 7 kicker. I’m out of position vs an aggressive button who opened wide preflop and will have lots of A‑x, T‑x and broadway combos that dominate my hand. With deep stacks I don’t want to bloat the pot OOP with a marginal middle pair. Checking controls the pot, lets me see how he reacts, and keeps the door open to call a reasonable C‑bet (realizing equity vs bluffs/draws) but fold to heavy pressure. Plan: check and evaluate his flop bet size — call small/medium c‑bets (~⅓–½ pot, roughly 200–300) to realize equity, fold to large overbets or strong turn aggression, and re‑assess on the turn.

Heads-up on the button facing a check after preflop raise got called. Flop As5sTd is ace-high with a spade flush draw possible and some gutshot straight draws (including mine for 7), but overall fairly dry and disconnected. My 68dd missed completely but has backdoor straight and flush potential. As preflop raiser in position with a polarized range (Ax, Tx, draws, bluffs like suited connectors), a standard continuation bet sizes down the pot and takes it often against BB calling range which includes many Ax that may check-fold or worse hands. Pot 600, bet 1/2 pot (300) maintains balance, applies pressure, and keeps SPR manageable with my 10k stack vs her 29k. Checking back risks giving free cards to draws/improvers and misses value/steal opportunities over 150-hand tournament where aggression maximizes EV.

Call 300. Elon’s small (~1/3) flop stab into a 900 pot looks like a wide heads‑up c‑bet range (Ax, Tx, broadways, and many bluffs). I have middle pair (5♦) with a weak 7 kicker — vulnerable but with real showdown value versus missed broadways. Calling 300 into 900 gives roughly 4:1 pot odds (need ~20% equity), which is easily met against a wide c‑bet range. I’ll avoid bloating the pot OOP by raising with a marginal hand; plan is to call and see the turn, fold to large turn aggression unless I improve (two pair/trips or a very favorable board), and re‑evaluate if he barrels small again or checks behind for showdown.

Check. I have a marginal middle pair (5s on board + my 5♦ = pair of fives) with a weak 7 kicker and I’m out of position on a dangerous turn that brings a third spade (A♠ 5♠ T♦ 9♠). Elon has been opening and c‑betting wide, so his range contains lots of A‑x, T‑x and two‑spade combos that now beat me or have big equity. There’s no bet to call, so I prefer pot control: keep the pot small, see how he reacts, and preserve chips. Plan: if he checks I go to showdown; if he bets a small/medium amount I’ll call to realize equity vs bluffs and missed broadways; if he barrels big or applies heavy pressure I’ll fold unless I have a strong read or improve to two‑pair/trips.

Heads-up on the button after preflop raise and flop c-bet both got called by Minni in the BB. Turn 9s adds a third spade, completing some flush draws and bringing gutshot straight draws (my 68dd has a gutshot to the 7 for a 6-7-8-9-T straight, ~10% equity to hit, plus live backdoor diamond flush draw). Board is draw-heavy (spade flush possible, straight draws like 67, JQ), but as the preflop raiser who c-bet flop, my range is polarized: strong Ax (A5s+, AT+, suited broadways), sets/two-pair (AA,55,TT? unlikely, A5/T9 unlikely), combo draws, and semi-bluffs like suited connectors/gappers that missed (like mine). Minni's check-call flop range on As5sTd likely includes weak Ax (A2s-A4s, offsuit A5/T low), Tx suited/spade draws, pairs/draws; turn check suggests weakness or trapping but history shows she's passive-cally (folds some turns/rivers but stations big spots). Pot 1200, my stack 9880 vs her 28920 gives room for aggression without bust risk. Checking back gives free river card to her draws/improvers and misses fold equity/value from her Ax/pairs that fold to pressure. Standard continuation bet ~2/3 pot (800) balances range, denies equity to her draws (spade flush draws have ~35% equity free card), gets value from weaker Ax/pairs, and fold equity from missed draws/medium strength (~40-50% fold equity vs her range). Maintains initiative in position over tournament; realizes max EV by pressuring passive defender who defends wide pre/flop but folds turns often in history.

I have a marginal middle pair (5♦) with 7♠ giving me four spades visible (As,5s,9s + my 7s) — I need one spade on the river for a flush. Outs: 9 spades + 3 sevens + 2 remaining fives = 14 outs. Probability to improve on the river ≈ 14/46 = 30.4%. Pot math: Elon’s turn bet makes the pot 2,000; calling 800 would make the final pot 2,800, so my break‑even equity is 800/2800 ≈ 28.6%. My raw equity to improve (and win or at least have reasonable showdown equity vs a wide button range) slightly exceeds the required pot odds, so a call is +EV here. Raising OOP with a one‑pair hand is thin — I’d be building a big pot for hands that largely dominate me (A‑x, T‑x, or hands that already have a spade combo), and Elon will often call or reraise with better holdings. Plan: call 800 to realize equity and see the river; if I miss and Elon applies heavy pressure (large shove or very big bet), fold unless I have a strong read; if I hit a flush/two‑pair/trips on the river, extract value.

I missed the river spade and only have a lone pair of fives with a weak 7 kicker. Elon opened and barreled flop/turn but checked the river, which looks like pot control or a weak/medium hand that still often beats me (A‑x, T‑x, paired overcards, or the occasional two‑spade combo). Betting here has very little fold equity — he already called two streets — and will largely be called by better hands while folding very little worse, so a bluff is thin EV. With no bet to face the lowest‑variance, most +EV line is to check to showdown and preserve chips; if he instead raises I’ll fold this marginal holding.

River decision heads-up vs Minni on button after leading preflop, flop c-bet 300 (pot ~600), turn bet 800 (pot ~1200), both called, now pot 2800, stacks 9080 vs 28120. Board As5sTd9s8c is draw-heavy (spade flush, 5-6-7-8-9/6-7-8-9-T straights possible with 67s/etc.). My 68dd missed everything until river 8c gives middle pair (eights) with 6 kicker, ahead of bluffs/missed draws/5x/Jx/QJ but behind Ax/Tx/9x/flushes/straights/two-pair+. As preflop raiser/consistent bettor, range polarized strong (Ax/Tx/spades/straights/combos) vs her check-call range (likely weak Ax like A-low/spadey, Tx, trapped monsters). Her river check after check-calls suggests weakness (Ax checking to pot control/induce, missed draws, avoids raise), not trapping nuts. History shows Minni passive/stationy (calls big pots medium strength, e.g., Hand 3/9/33/70/80) but folds some rivers/turns. Value bet thin for 3/4 pot (~2100, sized down from turn for balance) extracts from Ax/Tx/5x she calls, fold equity vs air/Jx, denies equity realization. Check-back risks free showdown win vs weak but misses EV+ value/FE in 150-hand tourney to max stack vs defender. 68dd blocks some straights (67)/two-pair (98), good thin value spot maintaining aggression.

I have only a marginal one‑pair (5♦) with a weak 7 kicker and I’m out of position. Elon opened wide preflop, c‑bet the flop, barreled the turn (when the third spade hit) and now fires a river raise of 2,100 into a 4,900 pot. Calling 2,100 to win ≈7,000 needs about 30% equity (2,100/7,000 ≈ 30%). My hand is behind most realistic value parts of his line (A‑x, T‑x, 9‑x, completed two‑spade combos, sets/two‑pair) and only ahead of a subset of missed broadways and pure bluffs. I explicitly planned to check to showdown and fold to a river raise with this marginal holding. Given the line, bet sizing and the stack dynamics, folding is the lowest‑variance, EV‑protecting play — preserve my large stack for better spots rather than calling into a range that often dominates me.

Heads-up on the button (SB) with Ad7h — a strong heads-up hand and a great spot to take the initiative. The pot is 150 and it only costs me 50 to call, but I prefer to raise and leverage my position. A min-raise to 200 is a standard, low-risk pressure play to steal the BB and build the pot when I'm likely ahead of Elon's wide calling/opening range. If he calls I can play aggressively in position; if he 3-bets big I'll usually fold this marginal hand rather than inflate a big pot out of position. With deep stacks I could size differently, but 200 balances risk and reward here.

Heads-up situation against Minni on the button/SB who min-raised to 200. Pot is 300, calling just 100 more for excellent implied odds (~3:1 direct). J6o is a playable hand heads-up with a jack blocker, straight potential, and decent equity (~38-42% vs. a typical wide button min-raise range of 40-60% hands). Deep stacks (118bb effective for me) allow seeing flops OOP profitably, as we can realize equity postflop. Folding would be too tight in HU; prefer calling over 3-betting a marginal hand without great blockers or playability. Will reassess postflop based on board texture and her action history (aggressive continuation betting).

Heads-up pot of 400 on a dry Ah Ts 4d flop (rainbow, ace-high with no flush draws). OOP Minni checked, which is standard with some Ax, Tx, or air/draws in her wide SB min-raise range, but often traps strong hands or pot-controls medium strength. My J6o (Js6c) is jack-high with a spade backdoor (matching Ts), but no pair, no strong draw—equity ~35-40% vs her checking range (blocks some JT/QJ but vulnerable to Ax/Tx). Deep effective stacks (~110bb), but as OOP caller pre, checking back realizes equity cheaply, avoids bloating pot OOP with marginal hand, and induces bluffs on turns where I can improve (e.g., 9 for OESD, spade flush draw). Betting thin risks check-raise from Ax or overcards; her history shows aggression but folds to c-bets sometimes—prefer pot control and free card here to maximize EV long-term in tournament.

I have Ad7h — top pair but a vulnerable kicker. Heads‑up on the button I opened preflop and Elon merely called; he then checked the flop Ah‑Ts‑4d. This is a very dry, rainbow board with almost no realistic draws, and my hand fares well against a wide calling range (we beat Kx/Qx/most Tx showdown floats and worse aces). Betting here earns value from worse aces and tens, denies free cards, and leverages my position. I’ll make a standard half‑pot c‑bet (200 into the 400 pot) — big enough to charge hands that improve on the turn but small enough to keep the pot controllable against a range that can check‑raise. If he calls I’ll re‑evaluate on the turn; if he check‑raises big I’ll strongly consider folding this marginal kicker hand out of position.

Heads-up vs Minni's wide SB min-raise range after my BB call with J6o. Flop AhTs4d is somewhat coordinated (backdoor flush/straight draws) but dry ace-high. I checked OOP for pot control with jack-high no pair/no draw. Her small 200 bet into 400 (half-pot) suggests a polarized range: value (Ax/Tx sets) or thin value/protection (Kx/Qx), mixed with bluffs air/draws from her loose range. Pot odds excellent at 3:1 direct (200 to 800 pot), plus deep stacks/implied odds for any improvement (e.g., J/T/6/9x turn). Equity ~35-45% vs her c-bet range due to J blocker (blocks some JT overpairs). Folding too tight heads-up; raising OOP with marginal blocker-only hand risks bloating pot vs better Ax or getting called by worse floats. Calling maintains pot control, realizes equity cheaply, induces turn bluffs when I hit or she misses, and keeps her honest vs our occasional traps. Consistent with prior flop check strategy and her aggressive history where she cbets wide but folds to resistance sometimes.

Heads-up on the turn vs Minni's wide SB min-raise range after calling pre and flop c-bet with J6o (now improved to middle pair sixes with J kicker on Ah Ts 4d 6s). Board brings a spade, completing backdoor flush draw and possibly improving some hands/draws like 97s, 75s, or Ax with spade. Pot 800, acting first OOP deep (~115bb eff). Middle pair is decent equity (~45-55% vs her polarized c-bet range: Ax/Tx/sets value, draws/bluffs air), blocks some Jx/TJ but vulnerable to top pair Ax/Tx, two-pair, straights (e.g., 75). Betting OOP risks check-raise from better hands/draws, thin value from worse unlikely to stack off, and folds out bluffs we dominate. Checking induces bets from her wide range (missed draws/value thin/protection), allows pot control/realizing equity cheaply, traps her air/medium strength, and sets up river play if I lead or she bets small. Consistent with prior passive flop strategy to realize equity postflop in deep HU tournament spots. Will call small-medium bets, fold to large overbets, and reassess river dynamics.

I have top pair (Ad7h) on a very dry Ah‑Ts‑4d‑6s board heads‑up. Elon checked both flop and turn after calling my flop c‑bet, so his range is wide (weaker aces, Tx, Kx/Qx, maybe some floating hands). This is a clear value/protection spot: there are almost no strong draws to worry about, and a half‑pot bet will get called by worse aces and tens while pricing out any backdoor equity. I’ll bet a standard half‑pot (400 into 800) to extract value and keep the pot controllable. If Elon check‑raises large I’ll heavily weigh folding the marginal kicker; if he calls I’ll re‑evaluate on the river and proceed cautiously given my weak kicker.

Heads-up on turn vs Minni's wide SB min-raise range (40-60% hands) after passive pre/flop play with J6o, now improved to middle pair of sixes (6c Js on Ah Ts 4d 6s board) with decent J kicker. Pot 1200, facing her 1/2-pot bet of 400 OOP after our mutual checks (pot control strategy). Her twice small c-bets suggest polarized range: strong Ax/Tx/sets/straights (75 for wheel), thin value KQ/JT, or bluffs/draws (spades now completed: A/Ts flush draws, 75 straight). Middle pair blocks some 6x/T6/J6, equity ~45-55% vs her c-bet range (vulnerable to Ax/Tx/75 but beats bluffs/air/draws). Pot odds excellent: 400 into 1200 (3.3:1 direct, ~25% needed), plus implied for river value if J/T/6/river pairs board or she bluffs. Deep stacks (~110bb eff) favor calling OOP to realize equity cheaply, control pot size, induce bluffs on blank rivers (we have SDV), and avoid raising OOP into better hands/draws (e.g., check-raise risk from nuts). Folding too tight HU; raising thin with one-pair OOP bloats pot unnecessarily when called by better or folds worse. Consistent with prior passive realization strategy vs aggressive opponent who cbets wide but shows down medium occasionally. Call and evaluate river: lead/fold to small bets if ahead, call medium if history suggests bluffs, shove/fold large.

River heads-up OOP with 6c Js on Ah Ts 4d 6s 4c board. Improved to two pair (sixes and fours using board pair of 4s and our 6c with 6s), ace kicker. Strong hand vs Minni's wide SB min-raise + two small c-bet range (Ax/Tx/pairs/draws/bluffs from 40-60% hands). Beats all one-pair hands (Ax=pair aces, Tx=pair tens), lower pairs/air; loses narrowly to 66 (full house), 44 (quads), A4 (trips 4s+). Paired low card river caps draws (spades turn completed, now club pairs 4), no flush/straight danger. Her small flop/turn cbets consistent with medium strength/polarization, likely calls value with Ax/Tx (~20-30% range). Pot 1600, deep eff ~110bb (my 11k). OOP first: value bet 3/4 pot (1200) extracts max from worse pairs/overcards/draws, denies equity to floats, builds pot for river. Checking induces bluffs/small value bets (can raise/call) but risks check-behind from cautious Ax/Tx, missing value in passive OOP line we've established. Consistent with prior pot control but upgrades to value now with nuts-adjacent hand in HU tournament spot to maximize stack. Sizing small-med avoids over-folding bluffs, gets 1-2 streets value effectively.

Elon leads the river for 1,200 into a 2,800 pot — I must call 1,200 to win ~4,000, so I need ~30% equity. I hold Ad7h: top pair but a very vulnerable kicker on Ah‑Ts‑4d‑6s‑4c. Elon's line (check‑call flop, check‑call turn, lead river) is consistent with a polarized set of hands: slow‑played strong holdings that now make a full house/trips (pocket 4s/quads, or TT turned into tens full), but also thinner value bets (worse Aces, some Tens) and occasional river bluffs. Given the dry texture, the bet size, and my positional/control of the pot earlier, I beat a large portion of his calling range and I’m getting correct pot odds to call. Raising would only extract thin value from worse Aces/Tens and unnecessarily inflate the pot against hands that crush me; folding surrenders too much equity for a small price. I’ll call 1,200 and go to showdown.

Heads-up on the button (SB) vs Minni in BB with K7o. This is a playable hand in position with initiative. Standard opening range from button heads-up includes K7o. Raising to 3x the BB (300 total) builds the pot, takes advantage of position postflop, and applies pressure. Calling the 50 is too passive and gives up initiative; folding is too tight. Minni's stack is deep, but effective stacks are sufficient for postflop play.

I’m in the big blind with A♣T♦ facing Elon’s button raise to 300. ATo is a playable heads‑up hand but I’m out of position postflop and Elon has shown frequent aggression. 3‑betting would bloat the pot and could induce a shove from his shorter stack with hands that dominate me (Ax), so I’d rather keep the pot controlled and see a flop. I’ll call the extra 200 (total 300) and proceed cautiously on the flop — continue with top pair, strong draws or good textures, fold to heavy aggression on dry misses. With my large chip lead there’s no need to force variance preflop.

Check. On Q♦ K♠ 5♣ with A♣ T♦ I have only ace‑high and a one‑card gutshot to Broadway (any J completes a straight, ~16% by the river). Elon opened preflop and is frequently aggressive, so he will c‑bet a wide range; I don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal hand. With a large chip advantage I’ll keep the pot controlled: check to induce bluffs and call reasonable continuation bets to realize equity, folding or conceding to very large/ polarizing pressure. If the turn improves me (or gives clear bluffing opportunities) I can take the initiative then.

Heads-up on the flop with top pair (kings) and a 7 kicker on a Qd Ks 5c board. The board is relatively dry with no flush draws completed and limited straight draws (possible 67 or JT gutshots at best). I raised pre-flop and have position, so Minni's check suggests weakness or a trapping hand, but her calling range includes worse pairs, Ax, suited connectors, etc. Top pair warrants a value/protection c-bet here to charge draws and build the pot. Sizing 400 (2/3 pot) balances value extraction from worse hands while keeping pot manageable with my 9580 effective stack vs. her deeper stack. Checking back risks giving free cards to overcards or draws.

I have A♣T♦ on Q♦ K♠ 5♣ — only ace‑high with 7 clean outs (3 Aces + 4 Js). The pot is 1,000 and Elon bet 400; calling 400 gives me ~28.6% break‑even equity, and my exact chance to improve by river is ≈27.9% — very close. Elon has shown frequent aggression and will c‑bet a wide, often weak heads‑up range, so I prefer to keep the pot controllable and realize equity rather than bloat the pot with a marginal hand. Raising here would be high variance (and can be crushed by Ax/Kx) while folding is too tight against an aggressive c‑bet. Plan: call to see the turn — if I pair my Ace or hit a J I can value bet or extract maximally; if I miss and face a large, polarizing barrel I’ll fold to avoid losing a big pot unnecessarily. With my large stack advantage there’s no reason to force variance now.

Board: Qd Ks 5c Qh. I hold A♣T♦ — only ace‑high with a one‑card Broadway straight draw (any J). There are 3 clean A outs and 4 J outs, but Jacks are not clean if Elon holds a queen (board paired), so my real equity is limited. Elon opened preflop and c‑bet the flop, showing a wide/aggressive range that includes Kx, Qx, Ax and many bluffs. With a 29k vs 9.2k stack advantage there is no need to bloat the pot with a marginal hand. Checking keeps the pot under control, lets me realize equity to a cheap river, and induces bluffs I can call or raise selectively on later streets. If Elon bets the turn I'll evaluate sizing: call smaller bets to try to hit my outs, fold to large, polarized pressure. Betting/raising now would be high variance and often get called or re‑raised by better hands, so check is best.

Heads-up on the turn with top pair kings (weak kicker) on a paired Qd Ks 5c Qh board. Pot 1400, villain checks again after calling flop. Her range includes Qx (now improved to two pair or better), weaker Kx, Ax (overcards/draws), pocket pairs below Q, or bluffs. My hand has significant showdown value and is ahead of much of her Ax, 5x, JTs type hands that called flop. Betting extracts value from worse pairs/draws, protects against gutshots (e.g., JT, A4), and denies equity. Checking risks giving free cards to overcards or inducing only bluffs on river. Sizing to ~70% pot (1000) balances value/thin value while keeping SPR reasonable (~6:1 post-bet). If raised, can fold to likely better hands given weak kicker. Deep stacks but this spot warrants aggression in position.

Call 1000. With A♣T♦ on Qd Ks 5c Qh I have ~7 outs (3 A + 4 J) — roughly 27–28% to improve by the river. The pot is 2400 and calling 1000 gives me about 29.4% break‑even equity, so this is borderline but reasonable given Elon’s frequent aggression and wide heads‑up c‑betting range (he will have a lot of Kx/Qx and bluffs). I have a huge stack advantage (≈29.4k vs 8.18k) so there’s no need to bloat the pot or create unnecessary variance by raising; calling keeps the pot controlled and allows me to realize equity and induce bluffs. Plan: call now; if I pair an Ace or hit a Jack I’ll extract value on the river (or call a shove), if I miss I’ll be prepared to fold to large polarized river overbets.

The river J♠ completes T‑J‑Q‑K‑A: my A♣T♦ now makes Broadway — the nut straight vs most of Elon’s range. Elon was the preflop/button aggressor and barreled flop/turn, but checked the river; that check makes heavy full‑house/quads holdings (QQ/KK/KQ/JJ/5x full houses) less likely and increases the weight of top‑pair/two‑pair and marginal value/bluff combos that will call a value bet. I should be getting value rather than giving it up by checking. I’ll size to extract from Kx/Qx/Jx/two‑pair types while not over‑betting into the small subset of hands that beat me. Betting 2000 into a 3400 pot (~60% pot) balances getting value and keeping weaker hands in. Contingency: if Elon check‑raises big (polarized/all‑in), that range is heavily weighted to full houses/quads and I’ll fold; I’ll call modest resistance but refuse to bloat the pot against a large polarizing raise.

On the river facing a 2000 bet into 5400 from Minni after she called my flop and turn continuation bets with K7o giving me top pair weak kicker. The board Qd Ks 5c Qh Js is paired queens, with possible strong hands like Qx (two pair or trips), KJ (kings + queens), KQ (same), or sets. My weak kicker makes K7 vulnerable to any better Kx or Qx. Her range that double-called preflop, flop c-bet, and turn bet before leading river is polarized towards value (Qx, KQ, KJ, sets) over bluffs or missed draws, as positionally she checked twice and now leads. Pot odds are ~3.7:1 but equity likely <25% against her strong leading range. Stacks allow fold without regret; raising risks 4000+ into likely monster while thin value calls get called by better. Consistent with turn plan to fold to resistance given kicker. Folding preserves stack for better spots (hand 88/150, deep effective).

Heads-up on the button, QTo is a strong open-raising hand. With pot 150 and only 50 to call, a min-raise to 200 (2x BB) applies pressure on the big blind, gains initiative, and wins the pot a lot of the time while keeping the pot manageable with deep stacks. Elon (BB) has ~80BB and shows aggression sometimes, so I’ll raise to take the lead; plan is to fold to a large 3-bet but continue postflop in position if he calls.

Heads-up against Minni on the button/SB who min-raised to 200, building a pot of 300. My stack is 8080 (~80BB deep), hers is massive at 31620. Minni opens wide from SB in heads-up spots based on history of frequent raises with marginal hands. Kc8d is a strong blocker hand here: king blocks premium king hands in her range, and 8 gives decent high-card strength vs random. Post-flop playability is okay with potential for top pair top kicker or draws. Getting great pot odds to call but prefer 3-betting for fold equity (SB folds wide to 3bets per history) and to build pot when ahead vs her wide range. Equity analysis: ~45-50% vs typical SB open range. Sizing to 600 (3x her raise) balances value, thin value, and semi-bluff. Takes initiative and realizes equity well.

Elon 3-bet my 200 open to 600. QTo is a decent heads-up hand and plays very well in position — I’ve got a big stack advantage so there’s no need to bloat the pot with a thin 4-bet. Calling keeps the pot controllable, lets me realize equity and use position postflop. I’ll call the extra 400 (600 total committed) and fold to extreme action preflop or to heavy, non‑showdown aggression on unfavorable flops.

Monster hand with trips (8d completing the pair of 8s on board) and king kicker on a dry paired board (8c Ts 8h). Minni checked SB first postflop after calling my preflop 3bet, representing possible Ax, broadway, suited connectors, overpairs, or Tx two pair. This is a strong value spot; betting extracts from worse hands like top pair (T or K), overpairs (JJ-QQ, AA unlikely post-3bet call), weaker two pairs, and any draws (97, J9 for gutshots/straights). Large sizing protects against Tx and combo draws while maximizing value from her calling range. 900 (75% pot) balances value, pot-building, and fold equity against pure air. Deep stacks (75BB effective) support aggressive line continuation from preflop 3bet.

I have top pair (T♥) with a Q♠ kicker on 8♣ T♠ 8♥ — a very strong heads‑up holding in position. Preflop Elon 3‑bet to 600 and now c‑bet 900 into a 1,200 pot (pot is 2,100). Calling 900 (1500 total committed from me) is the correct line: it keeps the pot controlled, realizes my position advantage, and extracts value from worse hands (A‑x, K‑x, many Tx, and bluffs) while avoiding bloating the pot vs overpairs/trips/quads. There are almost no meaningful draws on this board, so protection via a raise is unnecessary and risks a bigger confrontation (shove or a re‑raise) where I could be behind. Calling gives me the option to re‑evaluate on the turn with position — raise or bet for value if he shows weakness, fold if he shows clear strength. I’ll call.

Monster hand with top trips and king kicker on a paired board with a low spade turn card. The 2s adds spade flush draws but doesn't complete straight draws or improve many hands beyond two pair or top pair (Tx, 8x). As preflop 3-bettor and flop bettor into a calling station (Minni frequently calls large bets down in history, e.g., Hands 3,7,9,33,51, etc.), continue aggressive value line to extract from her range of Tx (top pair/good kicker), weaker trips (pocket 8s unlikely, but 8x possible), overpairs, and any draws. Boat combos like T8 or TT/88 are possible but in minority of her 3bet-call + flop-call range. Pot 3000, effective stacks deep (66BB), bet ~75% pot (2250) builds pot while leaving room for river jam if called. Protects against unlikely gutshots (J9) and induces bluffs from missed draws.

I call. I have top pair (T♥) with a Q kicker in position on a very dry board (8♣ T♠ 8♥ 2♠). Elon 3‑bet preflop and barreled the flop, then bets 2,250 on the turn — his line is consistent with both value (8x, TT, strong Ax/Kx/Tx) and aggressive continuation bluffs. The 2♠ is a blank and there are few completed draws, so raising here would just bloat the pot and risk isolating myself against sets/full houses; folding would be too nitty with a high‑equity, IP holding. Pot maths: I must call 2,250 to win ~7,500 (≈30% break‑even equity), and my hand has well above that vs his continuing range. I’ll call (total committed 3,750) to keep worse hands and bluffs in, control the pot with position, and re‑evaluate on the river — bet for value if he shows weakness, fold to a polarized shove if the river action clearly represents a better made hand.

Monster trips with king kicker on a paired board where the river 9d completes some gutshot straights (e.g., J7 for 7-8-9-T-J) and adds a diamond, but doesn't complete obvious flushes or heavily improve ranges. Minni's line—calling preflop 3bet, flop call, turn call into my strong range—suggests Tx (top pair good kicker), weaker 8x (e.g., 8Q, 87), overpairs (JJ-QQ, unlikely AA/KK post-call), two pairs (T9 new on river, possible), or draws that missed/bricked. Full house combos like T8, 88, TT are possible but capped lower in her range given history of calling without monsters often (e.g., Hands 9,33,51,70,80 where she calls big with worse). As preflop 3-bettor and barrel aggressor, continue value betting thin; her calling station tendencies (frequent large calls river) warrant max value via all-in shove (4430 into 7500, ~60% pot). Gets called by worse trips/two pair/overpairs while superior to most; stacks shallow effective (44BB), denies equity to draws, maximizes EV vs her loose calls. Checking risks giving free showdown to worse or small pot.

I have top pair (T♥) with a Q kicker on a very dry board (8♣ T♠ 8♥ 2♠ 9♦). Elon has shoved all‑in for 4,430 into a pot of 11,930; calling requires ~27% equity (4,430 / (11,930+4,430) ≈ 27%). The river 9♦ is a blank — it doesn’t complete any plausible straight/flush and only slightly helps a few specific combos. Hands that beat me are full houses/quads (88, 8T, TT, 88x combos) which are possible but relatively infrequent; many hands in his continuing range (A‑x, K‑x, missed broadways, some bluffs) are behind. Given the pot odds, my hand’s showdown value, and position, a call is +EV. I can’t raise (he’s all‑in). Total I’ll have committed after calling: 3,750 (already in) + 4,430 = 8,180 chips.

Pocket nines is an extremely strong hand heads-up pre-flop, ranking near the top of starting hands. As the button/SB, I have positional advantage post-flop. Minni is in the BB with a larger stack, but given the deep stacks and my goal to maximize EV over many hands, raising standard size to isolate and build the pot while denying equity to her wide calling range is optimal. Her history shows she limps/calls often but folds to pre-flop raises frequently when out of position. Raising to 3x the BB (300 total) is a balanced size that gets value from worse hands and folds out junk. Folding or limping would be a mistake with this strength.

I have Q♠7♣ in the big blind facing a single-button open to 300. With deep stacks and Elon opening frequently in heads-up, Q7o has reasonable equity and the price is cheap (200 more into a 400 pot). I'm out of position, so I don't want to 3-bet a marginal offsuit queen — calling keeps the pot manageable and lets me see a flop where I can continue with top pair or strong draws and fold to heavy pressure on bad textures. I'll call the raise and decide based on the flop.

I called preflop for price but on this flop (A♥ T♣ 3♣) I have only queen-high with one club (7♣) — no pair and only a very weak backdoor flush possibility that needs running clubs. Elon was the preflop raiser and will c-bet a high frequency here, and I’m out of position, so I don’t want to build the pot or bluff-blast with such a marginal holding. Checking controls the pot, preserves stack depth, and lets me see how he behaves: I’ll call small continuation bets to realize equity and fold to heavy pressure if I don’t improve; if he checks back I can consider a delayed turn plan. For these reasons I check and keep my options open for the turn.

Pocket 9s is an overpair on a flop of 3c Tc Ah, which is somewhat coordinated with a flush draw and possible straight draws, but no Ace in my hand blocks some of her strong Ax ranges. I raised pre-flop from the button, and Minni called from the BB, giving her a wide range including Ax, pairs below 9s, suited connectors, and draws. Minni checked, which is standard from OOP. With position and a strong but vulnerable hand, I should bet for value against worse Ax (like AQo, AJo), overcards, draws, and floats, while protecting against her calling range that includes flush/straight draws. Checking risks giving free cards to draws and showdown value to weaker hands. A bet of around 2/3 pot (400 into 600) balances value, protection, and fold equity without overbetting into nuts. Her history shows she calls flops often but folds to continued aggression when missing. Deep stacks allow this play to maximize EV long-term.

Board A♥ T♣ 3♣, I hold Q♠7♣ and I checked to control the pot OOP. Elon c-bets 400 into a 1000 pot — calling 400 requires ~28.6% equity. I have 6 clean outs (3 queens + 3 sevens) and ~24% to hit by the river, slightly below raw pot‑odds, but heads‑up Elon c‑bets a wide range and will include bluffs/weaker hands. With deep stacks I prefer a pot‑control check‑call: realize equity, keep the pot manageable, and fold to heavy turn pressure if I don’t improve. I’ll call the 400 and re-evaluate on the turn (raise only with a real improvement or a very specific read).

I have Q♠7♣ on A♥ T♣ 3♣ / A♦. I’m out of position and unpaired with no realistic draw (the turn was a diamond, so the backdoor club flush is gone). I only have 6 clean outs (3 queens + 3 sevens) — insufficient to take an aggressive line here against a preflop raiser who c‑bet the flop and will frequently have an Ace or better on this paired‑Ace turn. Betting as a bluff is too thin and will either get called by worse hands or raise me off my equity; leading out here bloats the pot with practically no showdown value. With deep stacks I should preserve chips and keep the pot small, so I’ll check and evaluate the river action: fold to significant aggression, call small bets only when the price/river texture justifies it, and consider a river bluff only if the opponent gives a clear check‑back or a very weak range.

Pocket 9s remains a strong overpair heads-up, but the turn pairs the ace, making the board 3c Tc Ah Ad, which improves any Ax in Minni's range to trips while completing some straight draws and keeping the club flush draw live. Minni's check-call on the flop (coordinated board) suggests a wide range including Ax (weak or medium), mid pairs, flush draws (clubs), gutshots (e.g., KJ, QJ, J9), and floats. Her turn check indicates possible weakness, as strong Ax hands might bet for value OOP. However, checking behind risks free cards to draws and showdown value to weaker Ax/pairs. With deep stacks (my 15k+ into 1.4k pot), position, and my aggressive pre-flop and flop action establishing a strong range, a continuation bet extracts value from worse hands (weaker Ax like AJo/KQo, 8s/7s, draws) while protecting against live draws. Minni's history shows she calls stations frequently but folds to multi-street aggression when behind (e.g., Hands 3,10,20,26). A pot-sized bet (1400) balances fold equity from bluffs/draws, thin value from capped hands, without overcommitting. This maximizes EV over remaining 60 hands by denying equity and building the pot when ahead.

Fold. The turn bet asks me to call 1400 into a 2800 pot (I must commit 1400 to win 4200 total), so I need ~33% equity. I only have 6 clean outs (3 queens + 3 sevens) to improve on the river: 6/46 ≈ 13% equity — far below the pot‑odds. Elon's line (preflop open, flop c‑bet, then a large turn continuation on a paired‑Ace) heavily favors value hands (A‑x, A10, sets, strong tens) and while some bluffs exist they’re unlikely to be frequent enough to make this a +EV call. Raising as a bluff OOP with Q7o is too thin and would only bloat a pot I’m a big dog in. I’ll preserve my 22.9k stack and wait for a better spot.

Heads‑up on the button with 6♦7♠: this is a playable, connected hand with good post‑flop potential and lots of fold equity in heads‑up. I want to take the initiative and play the hand in position rather than passively completing. A standard 2xBB open to 200 (200 total committed) pressures the big blind, keeps SPR reasonable given deep stacks, and lets me realize equity on favorable flops. Plan: if Elon folds I take the pot; if he calls I’ll use position to c‑bet appropriate flops and look to realize equity with straights/top pair/draws; if he 3‑bets large I can fold or re‑evaluate depending on sizing, and consider calling small 3‑bets given deep stacks. Raise to 200 to open the pot and play postflop in position.

Pocket Tens is an extremely strong hand heads-up, especially in the big blind facing a button min-raise from an aggressive opponent like Minni, who frequently opens wide from late position based on hand history (e.g., raises with marginal hands and folds to 3-bets in spots like Hands 3, 26, 27). Calling gives her position and allows her to realize equity with draws post-flop. 3-betting takes initiative, isolates, builds the pot with a premium hand, and pressures her wide range. Sizing to 700 (3.5x her raise) is standard for deep-stack HU, balancing value and fold equity while denying her cheap equity realization. Effective stacks are deep (~170BB), so no over-shove needed. Folding is never correct.

Facing Elon's 3‑bet to 700 after my 200 open, with 6♦7♠ on the button I prefer to keep the pot manageable and use my positional advantage. 6‑7 offsuit is too playable heads‑up to fold — it has decent straight potential and strong implied odds — and a light 4‑bet (min to 1200) would be unnecessarily committal versus an opponent who 3‑bets fairly wide. Calling 500 more (700 total committed) gives me reasonable pot odds (≈35–36% break‑even) and preserves stack depth to apply post‑flop pressure or realize equity. Plan: see the flop, c‑bet or float on favorable textures, and fold to overbearing aggression when appropriate.

Pocket Tens remains very strong heads-up on this flop of 9s Jh 4s. It's an overpair to the 9 and 4, and the board is draw-heavy with two spades (flush draw) and possible straight draws (e.g., T-Q, 10-8, 5-10 gutshots), but TT blocks some 10x gutshots and is ahead of Minni's 3-bet calling range, which includes overcards like AK/AQ, suited broadway (e.g., KQs, QJs which hit J or have spade draw), mid-pairs (88-55), and some sets (but low likelihood). As the pre-flop raiser, a continuation bet takes initiative, charges draws, protects against overcards/turns, and extracts value from worse pairs/hands that missed. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or floats. Sizing to 900 (roughly 2/3 pot) balances value from top of her range (worse pairs/draws) and fold equity from air/overcards, keeping pot manageable deep-stacked (~160BB). Minni's history shows she folds to post-flop aggression when behind (e.g., Hands 3,10,26,55), so this pressures her wide button defense.

Call 900. I have 6♦7♠ on 9♠ J♥ 4♠ — no pair but decent heads‑up post‑flop playability: backdoor straight/flush possibilities and I’m in position. Elon 3‑bet pre and now c‑bet 900 into ~1400 (pot 2300); calling 900 requires ~28% equity (900/(2300+900)) which is reasonable given his wide heads‑up range and the ability to float and realize equity on the turn. Raising here would bloat the pot with little raw equity and fold is too nitty versus a wide c‑bet. Plan: call to see the turn, then continue if I pick up a pair/draw or can credibly bluff; fold to heavy aggression on brick turns that don’t improve my story.

Pocket Tens remains a strong overpair on this turn board: 9s Jh 4s 5h. The turn 5h brings some straight draws (e.g., 67 for straight, gutshots like 36, 26 for wheel possibilities) and pairs the 5 for sets/trips, but TT blocks some 10x combinations and is still ahead of Minni's calling range from the flop c-bet, which includes Jx (AJ/KJ/QJ), 9x, lower pairs, spade flush draws (e.g., AsXs, KsQs), and combo draws. Her button 3-bet defense range is wide but capped somewhat by the call. No completed flush, and the heart doesn't complete many strong hands she likely has. As preflop aggressor and OOP, betting continues to take initiative, charges remaining draws (spades still live, new straight/heart draws), protects against overcards turning into pairs, and extracts value from floats, weaker Jx/9x, and trapped lower pairs/sets. Checking risks giving a free card to draws that can outdraw us ~20-30% time (e.g., spade draw has ~19% to river flush). Minni's history shows she calls flops but folds turns/rivers to continued aggression when behind (e.g., Hands 10,26,55,64,73,78,86), so a pot-sized bet builds the pot for value while applying pressure. Sizing to 3200 (pot-sized) maximizes fold equity from air/draws while getting called by worse (e.g., J9, J8s, 98s, 87s). Stacks deep (~48BB effective for her, 48BB for me), so no need to go smaller and risk multi-street play. This spot is highly +EV for thin value/bet-fold equity.

Elon just pot‑bet the turn for 3,200 into a pot that reads 6,400. Calling 3,200 would make the final pot 9,600 — I therefore need ~33.3% equity to break even. With 6♦7♠ on 9♠ J♥ 4♠ 5♥ I have no pair and only four clean outs (the four 8s) to make my straight (~4/46 ≈ 8.7% to hit on the river). There is effectively no realistic flush draw and my backdoor possibilities are negligible. A raise here would be a pure bluff risking a large portion of my stack against an opponent who has shown heavy aggression and a range weighted toward strong hands; that line is high variance and unlikely to be +EV. Given the pot odds, my hand equity, and the opponent’s line, folding and preserving my stack for a better spot is the correct play.

Heads-up on the button with KcJc, a premium suited broadway hand with excellent playability and showdown value. Standard play is to raise to build the pot and take initiative. Minni is in BB and has been aggressive historically, but this hand is strong enough to raise standard size of 3x the BB (300 total). Position allows control post-flop. Deep stacks support this.

Heads-up in the big blind with T9o. Elon opened to 300 (small raise to 3x), pot is 400 and I need 200 more to call.
