Heads-up on turn OOP with Qc9s (queen-high, minimal improvement with 9s backdoor spade but no strong draw/pair). Board Td4d8c6s is draw-heavy (diamond flushes live, spade flush draw improved slightly, straights like 75/J7/56 possible). Pot 800 deep (~24BB effective), my range OOP includes many float calls like this from flop. Minni's BTN/SB range polarized: strong made hands (88/T8/sets), draws (Adxd, 57s, J7s), bluffs (Axo, KQo). Qc blocks some Qx value (QJ/QT), 9s blocks some 97 straight draws. Equity ~30-40% vs her range, good for calling small bets but OOP first to act. Betting bluff risky: induces raises from her equity/draws/value, poor fold equity OOP HU deep. Thin value bet no (queen-high rarely best). Checking standard: controls pot, realizes equity/checks back vs bluffs/draws that miss, sets up check-call/raise river if improves or villain barrels weak. History shows Minni small-bets wide, folds to aggression sometimes; check keeps options open without bloating pot. Folding impossible, raising impossible.

I have top pair (T) with a weak 3-kicker (Tc3d) on Td-4d-8c-6s. I opened preflop and c-bet the flop; Elon called, showing a wide calling range (worse tens, middle pairs, overcards and floats). The turn 6s is a blank that didn’t complete the diamond draw, so this is a good spot to take the initiative for value and protection. Betting denies free cards to overcards, extracts value from worse tens/middling pairs, and still keeps the pot manageable with deep stacks. I’ll make a half-pot bet to accomplish that (~400 into 800). If Elon raises large I’ll give up my weak kicker; if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the river based on the card and his action.

Heads-up OOP on turn with Qc9s (queen-high, backdoor spade draw with 6s on board). Board Td4d8c6s is draw-heavy: diamond flush draws strong, open-enders like J7/57/95, wheel draws, pairs T8/86/64. Pot 1200, facing 400 (1/3 pot) bet from aggressive BTN Minni who small-bets wide postflop in history (often value-caps weak pairs/draws, bluffs Ax/KQo). Excellent pot odds (400 into 1600 = ~20% equity needed). Qc9s equity ~35-45% vs her polarized range (pairs 20-30% equity, draws ~50%, bluffs 70%+); blocks QJ/QT value. Deep effective stacks (~19k) support calling to realize equity/implied odds vs her missed draws. Folding too tight vs tiny sizing (would fold only <20% equity hands). Raising bluff poor OOP: low fold equity vs her capped range/calling station tendencies, invites shoves from draws/sets. Check-call keeps pot controlled, induces bluffs/checks river OOP, sets up cheap showdown or check-raise if villain barrels air. Consistent with prior small-ball float strategy this hand. Max EV long-term deep.

River OOP heads-up deep (~19k effective, ~190BB) with Qc9s (queen-high air, no pair/draw completed). Board Td4d8c6s2d completes obvious diamond flushes (Ad/Kd/Qd/Jd/3d/etc. from her BTN range), possible straights (53 for wheel unlikely OOP float range, J75/97 gutshots missed), pairs T8/86/64/42 improved weakly. My check-call flop/turn line reps polarized: missed diamond/spade draws (Axs, 75s, J9s), weak pairs (99-QQ capped), bluffs (KJo/AQo floats). Qc blocks Qx value (QT/QJ), 9s blocks 97 straight. Equity dismal (~10-20% vs value, 50%+ vs bluffs); showdown value minimal vs her aggressive small-bet range (history: wide c-bets flop/turn, barrels thin value/missed draws often). Betting bluff terrible OOP river: poor fold equity (she calls light/small sizings per history, snaps off with pairs/draws), risks raise-all-in from flushes/sets (~25% range), dead money minimal (no prior aggression). Thin value bet absurd (queen-high rarely best). Checking optimal: induces bluffs/value-caps from her wide range (Axo/KQo/A-high missed flushdraws, weak T8/86), gets free showdown vs air, pot control, consistent with small-ball float/ OOP passive strategy this hand. Folds her strongest hands pre-action; if bet faced, re-eval based sizing (small call, large fold). History confirms Minni checks back weak/mixed often OOP river. Maximizes EV long-term HU deep.

I have top pair (T♣3♦) on Td-4d-8c-6s-2d with a very weak 3‑kicker. Elon called my flop and turn bets (passive line) and then checked the river — that line is consistent with medium-strength hands (worse tens, 8x, small/mid pocket pairs, or floats). The river 2♦ is a relatively safe card — it only gives a flush to someone holding two diamonds in hand (unlikely), and hands that beat me are mostly tens with better kickers. With deep stacks I want thin value without bloating the pot or turning hands that call into bluffs. A small value bet (~1/3 pot) should get called by worse tens, 8x, 6x and other one-pair hands while folding out air. If Elon raises big I can fold this weak-kicker top pair. Betting 500 gives good value while controlling risk.

River OOP heads-up deep stacks (~190BB effective) with Qc9s (queen-high no pair/draw). Board Td4d8c6s2d completes diamond flushes (strong hands like AdKd/Jd5d/T9dd from BTN range), possible straights (75o/s unlikely in my float range), improved pairs (T9/Tx/8x/6x/4x/2x). Pot 2100, facing 500 bet (~1/4 pot) from Minni after her small c-bet flop/turn line (aggro BTN history: wide small bets postflop, often thin value/caps/missed draws, but calls down light). Pot odds excellent: 500 into 2600 (~19% equity needed). Qc9s ~25-35% equity vs polarized range (low vs value/flushes ~10-15%, high vs bluffs/missed draws like Axo/KQo/A-high flushdraws/57/J7 ~60-80%); blocks QJ/QT value somewhat, reps float/missed draw in my check-check-call line. Folding too tight vs tiny sizing/deep stacks (beats pure air/bluffs, realizes SDV vs thin value); raising bluff disastrous OOP river (low fold equity vs calling station tendencies/history, risks AIs from flushes/sets). Check-call maximizes EV: hero potential vs overvalue, induces light calls if value, consistent w/ small-ball float strategy this hand. History (e.g., Hand 80/88/94) shows Minni small-bets wide/rivers thin, but folds to pressure sometimes; call extracts vs bluffs, caps damage OOP.

Heads-up on the button/SB with deep stacks. T4o is a playable hand in this spot as part of a wide opening range (60%+ of hands are standard here). Minni defends wide based on history but folds often to preflop raises with weak holdings. Raising builds the pot when called or steals blinds frequently. Position post-flop is advantageous for realizing equity. Standard sizing: 3x the BB to 300.

Heads-up against a frequent opener: 7♦3♠ is garbage, but defending the big blind vs a 3x open is very wide. Calling 200 more (300 total including my 100 BB) is cheap relative to the 400 pot — I only need ~33% equity to break even. I’ll call to realize equity and keep the pot small; out of position I’ll play cautiously on the flop and fold to significant aggression if I miss. A 3-bet here with 7-3o is too speculative, and folding surrenders too much frequency, so call.

Heads-up out of position with 7♦3♠ on 8♠-6♥-3♣: I’ve hit a weak pair (a small pair with a poor kicker). Elon is a frequent opener and will have a very wide c-betting range here, but he also bets/raises a lot postflop — so I want to control the pot and keep options open rather than bluff‑shipping. Checking preserves stack size, lets me call modest continuation bets from his wide range (realizing fold equity and extracting value from bluffs/overcards), and fold comfortably to large aggression. Plan: check now; call small-to-medium cbets (roughly up to ~1/2 pot) to realize equity, fold to big overbets or heavy turn pressure, and re-evaluate on the turn if I improve or the action is passive.

Preflop raiser in position heads-up with initiative after Minni checks. Board is 6h-8s-3c: somewhat coordinated with possible straight draws (e.g., 4-5-7, 5-7-9) and a spade, but dry enough for wide c-betting. I have 7♦3♠ on 8♠-6♥-3♣ — a small pair (bottom pair) with a poor kicker, out of position. Elon is a frequent opener and just made a small flop bet (300 into a 900 pot), which typically represents a wide c-betting range (overcards, floats, small pairs, and bluffs). I have bottom pair (3s) with a weak kicker, out of position, on a 6h-8s-3c-Td board. Elon is a frequent opener and aggressive postflop — the T on the turn now helps a lot of his preflop raising range (Tx, broadway pairs), so I don’t want to inflate the pot with a marginal holding. Plan: check now; call small-to-medium bets (roughly up to ~1/2 pot) and fold to big overbets or heavy raises; re-evaluate on the river if I improve or the action stays passive.

On the turn, the 10d pairs my Th, giving me top pair (tens) with a weak 4s kicker. The board (6h-8s-3c-Td) has straight draw potential (e.g., 45, 57, 79, J9, J7) and a spade flush draw, but no flush completed. Deep stacks (17860 effective), position: betting extracts value from worse (e.g., 97o, 89o, draws) while protecting against runner-runner draws. Half-pot sizing (600 into 1200) balances value/protection without overbetting vs. her calling station tendencies seen in history (folds to turn aggression often but calls small bets). Checking risks giving free cards to draws or induces bluffs, suboptimal for maximizing stack.

I have 7♦3♠ — bottom pair (pair of threes) with a very weak kicker on 6♥-8♠-3♣-T♦. Raising would turn a marginal hand into a big pot where I’m likely dominated; folding would miss good pot odds. Plan: call 600, re-evaluate on the river — fold to large raises/overbets unless I improve to trips or see clear weakness, and call small‑to‑medium river bets to realize equity.

The river checked to me with the board 6♥-8♠-3♣-T♦-A♦ and I hold 7♦-3♠ — bottom pair with a terrible kicker. The A on the river improves many of Elon’s preflop/CB/turn value combos (Ax, Tx, two-pair) and he checked it back, which often means either a missed draw or a marginal holding willing to go to showdown. I’ll keep the pot under control and take my pair to showdown by checking.

River Ad completes no obvious draws (spade flush draw still open but unlikely given her check-call line; no broadway unless JQ specifically, narrow). (beats us if higher), weaker top pair like T9/T7/T5, 97/87 gutshots, or floats. Top pair weak kicker (T4s) has good equity vs her range (~60-70% vs medium pairs/draws per equity calcs). Half-pot 1200 balances value/protection, reps strength as PFR c-betting all streets, denies free showdown to bluffs/draws. I have bottom pair (3♠) with a poor kicker on A♦‑T♦‑8♠‑6♥‑3♣. Heads-up on the button with Qc2h. Pot is 150 and I only need to call 50, but in heads-up a queen-high offsuit is in my open-raising range from the button. I want to seize initiative and play the hand in position, while keeping the size small relative to deep stacks. I'll open to 300 (3BB) — a standard, constructive size that gives fold equity. 5h2d is a very weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity and playability postflop. Facing a button raise from Minni, who is aggressive but my hand has minimal equity against her wide range (dominated often by better low cards or suited hands). Pot odds are 200 to call into 400 (33% needed), but realization equity is far lower. No good blockers for bluffs. Deep stacks but near tournament end; preserve stack, fold trash.

Heads-up on the button (SB) with pocket 4s, a strong hand for this spot. Deep stacks (around 200BB effective). Elon opened to 300 from the button. I'm in the big blind with Kc6d and stacks are deep (~190bb). Heads-up the button's range is wide, so Kx has reasonable equity and postflop playability. A light 3-bet to 500 is possible as a bluff, but against an aggressive opponent that's likely to create bigger contests and I don't have the hand strength to take it down cleanly. Plan: call, play cautiously OOP — look to extract value with a king or good turns, fold to heavy aggression on unflattering runouts.

Heads-up out of position on a paired 9x board with Kc6d: I have only K-high and a very slim runner-runner club flush (needs both turn+river). Elon opened wide preflop, so he has many hands that will c‑bet this board, but I don’t have value or a reasonable draw to build the pot OOP on deep stacks. Pot control and information are paramount: check to see what Elon does. If he donates a small continuation bet I’ll consider a call to see a turn (small bets give correct pot odds); I’ll fold to large pressure or a check‑raise. If he checks back I get a free turn to improve or continue controlling the pot.

Heads-up on the button with 44 after raising preflop and getting called by Minni in BB. Flop 9h9c7c is paired, with a club flush draw, somewhat dry but coordinated. My range hits this well (overpairs, some 9x, sets, draws), while 44 has 6 outs to a set (~12% equity if behind) and blocks some low pairs. As preflop aggressor in position, c-betting ~2/3 pot (400) balances value (thin vs lower pairs/draws) with fold equity against air/missed draws. Checking back risks giving free cards to overcards/draws and forfeits my range advantage. Deep stacks allow continued aggression postflop if called. Bet sizing fits history of 300-600 flops.

I checked the flop with Kc6d on 9h-9c-7c. Elon c-bet a small amount (400) into a ~1000 pot. Board 9h 9c 7c 4s; I hold Kc6d — no pair, only a remote backdoor club. Elon opened wide pre and c‑bet a small 400 on the flop that I called, so his range can include air, weak pairs, and made 9s/7s. If he bets the river I’ll fold to large pressure unless I pair the King or get a very cheap small bet; if he checks back I’ll re-evaluate a small delayed bluff or a fold‑saving check depending on the river card.

Turn card 4s gives me top set with 44, an absolute monster on this 9h9c7c4s board—only beaten by extremely unlikely full houses (like 97 or 94) or quads. My preflop raising range and position justify continued aggression for maximum value. Deep stacks (~180BB effective) allow multi-street plans; betting extracts from draws/overcards that can call once and fold river if bricked, or worse made hands like two-pair/draw combos. Pot 1400, bet ~2/3 pot (1000) balances value (vs. draws/two-pair) and protection (vs. runner-runner draws), consistent with prior sizing (400 flop). Checking risks free river card to OESD/flush, forfeiting equity realization and range advantage. Opponent history shows calling stations vs. aggression but folding to repeated pressure without nuts.

Fold. After Elon's turn bet of 1000 the pot is 2400 and calling 1000 would make the pot 3400, so I need ~29.4% equity (1000/3400) to break even. Preserve my ~18.5k stack for better spots and fold this turn bet.

Heads-up on the button I should be opening a very wide range. T♣2♣ is weak but suited and has some playability postflop, so I’ll take the initiative. Heads-up preflop from the big blind facing a standard button min-raise to 300. My hand A6s suited is excellent for defense: ace-high with suited connectors offering strong playability, nut flush potential, and a strong ace blocker against opponent's button range, which is very wide (any two cards often). Calling is fine but allows opponent to realize equity cheaply; folding is a massive mistake. Standard 3-bet size to 3x the open (900 total) balances value (vs Ax, suited broadways) and bluffs. Consistent with maximizing EV over many hands by defending wide and exploiting button aggression seen in history.

Heads-up on the button I opened wide with T♣2♣. Preflop 3-bet with A6hh as BB vs button open is standard, polarizing range and taking initiative with suited ace playability. Checking folds out equity and gives free cards, suboptimal OOP. Bet to maximize EV by exploiting wide button defense.

Heads‑up on the button I hold T♣2♣ on 8♠ 8♣ T♦. After a preflop 3‑bet I have top pair (tens) with a very weak kicker and only a runner‑runner club possibility. Turn card 6d pairs my 6h, giving me bottom pair with Ace kicker on a paired 8s board (8s8cTd6d). Pot 3600, deep stacks (~16k effective), bet ~2/3 pot (2400) sizes well: gets value from 77-99/Tx worse pairs/Ax, fold equity vs air/draws that missed, builds pot for river if ahead, consistent with aggressive image from history (winning multiple pots postflop vs her calls). Avoids check-fold trap OOP; maximizes EV by denying equity and value-betting thin.

I have T♣2♣ on 8♠ 8♣ T♦ 6♦ — top pair with a very weak kicker. Pot is 6000 and Elon bets 2400; calling 2400 to win ~8400 requires ~22% equity. River 9c completes 6789T straights (67xx), pairs 9x into strong pairs/two pairs/trips if 99, and adds a club for potential blockers but no flush completes prominently (only two clubs total). My Ah6h improved to bottom pair + A kicker on turn (thin value then), but now faces river where most worse hands (pure air/missed draws like diam flushdraws Jd9d etc.) folded turn, and betters (Tx/9x/overpairs/straights) crush it. No significant fold equity left (she tank-called large turn IP); betting ~1/2-3/4 pot (4200-6000) extracts minimal thin value from rare worse (55-77 low pairs unlikely vs 3bet/call, pure AKo unlikely postflop calls) while called/raised by full value range (-EV). OOP check pot-controls, induces bluffs from capped range/missed draws (high with my image), cheap showdown vs medium strength that checks behind (e.g., QQ/KK fearing 88+/straights), preserves deep stack (~17k eff) for 31 remaining hands in HU. Hero-call if she bets small (blocker A6 strong vs her bluffs), fold to large. Optimal exploitation of her history calling big pots but folding to sustained aggression (e.g., Hands 3,10,24,26,27,44,51,55,64,70,73,78,86,88,90+ where callers folded river to pressure).

I have T♣2♣ on 8♠ 8♣ T♦ 6♦ 9♣ — top pair with a very weak kicker. Checking behind surrenders thin value; a small value bet should get called by worse hands while folding to a check‑raise (which will almost always be trips/quads/better tens). River 9c completes 6789T straights (67xx) and improves 9x to middle pair/two-pair potential on a paired 88 board with 6 and T. My A6hh turned into pair of 6s + A kicker (thin value bet spot turn), but now OOP check after aggression invites value from her nutted calling range (88+, 8x/Tx trips/two-pair, TT/99 full houses, 67 straight) built by calling 3bet + flop cbet + oversized turn bet IP. Her 2800 (~1/4 pot) bet after check looks like thin value or probe; bluffs (missed Kd/Jd/Td diam draws, gutshots to 7/J, A-high floats) possible but thinned out by turn call into me (perceived nuts-heavy range). Pair 6s crushed by 80%+ of her continuing range, beats <10% (missed draws unlikely post-turn, QQ/KK/AQ check-behind), pot odds 11200:2800 ~4:1 but equity <20% needed unmet long-term. History shows Minni calls big but folds rivers to pressure (3,10+); here her bet exploits my check w/ strong hands. Preserve ~17k stack for 31 HU hands vs loose Minni; folding marginal showdown maximizes EV avoiding -EV hero OOP.

Heads-up on the button (SB) with deep stacks (172 BB effective). 86o is a playable hand in a wide button opening range (~50-60% of hands in heads-up), offering post-flop playability with two overcards potential, straight possibilities, and position advantage. Elon (button/SB) opened to 300. I’m BB with T9♦ — a suited connector with excellent playability heads-up. I hold T♦9♦ on Q♥ 2♣ 7♣ — essentially a complete miss with only weak backdoor diamond/runner-runner straight possibilities. I'm out of position (BB) and the pot is 600. Elon is the button and a wide/aggressive opener, so betting OOP with air here would bloat the pot and put me in tough spots vs a player who C‑bets and barrels frequently. Heads-up on the flop with position and initiative as pre-flop raiser. Elon’s 300 into a 900 pot is a small (≈1/3 pot) c‑bet, giving me ~25% pot odds to call. T♦9♦ is a playable heads‑up hand — I have backdoor diamond flush and runner/runner straight possibilities and decent equity vs a wide c‑betting BTN range. Check. The turn (Q♥ 2♣ 7♣ 8♣) just made a three‑club board and I have T♦9♦ — no pair and no club. My raw equity to improve on the river is modest (~J or 6 for the straight, plus pairing 9/10 ≈ 14 outs ≈ 30% to see something), but many of the clean outs still lose to a club flush and I have essentially no showdown value right now. Elon is a wide, aggressive opener who barrels a lot; a bet OOP with T9 and no equity here just bloats a pot I’m unlikely to win and invites raises that I’ll have to fold to. Checking controls the pot, lets me realize my ~30% chance on the river, and keeps the initiative on Elon to bluff/assign a range first. Plan: check to the river; call small/medium bets with decent improving cards, fold to large river aggression unless I pair/complete the straight, and consider a river bluff only if the board/range dynamics are clearly favorable.

On the turn, the board is Qh-2c-7c-8c, and I hit top pair with 8d6h (weak kicker). Minni has checked both flop and turn after calling my flop c-bet, indicating possible weakness, a draw, or a mediocre hand like a queen or lower pair. With deep stacks (166 BB effective) and only 30 hands left, betting extracts value from worse pairs/draws, protects against runner-runner outs, maintains initiative, and builds the pot for river flexibility. Position allows control. Standard sizing: ~2/3 pot (800 into 1200) balances value and protection without overcommitting. History shows Minni calls light but folds to continued aggression often.

Call. Facing 800 into a 2000 pot means I need ~28.6% equity to break even. T9♦ has roughly 14 raw outs (pairs and J/6 straights ≈30% to improve), but several of those outs are clubs or lose to a made club flush — so effective equity is slightly degraded. Elon is a wide, aggressive opener who barrels a lot, so there’s a meaningful chance he’s barreling with weaker hands or single-club combos that don’t currently have a flush. Raising OOP with no pair is unnecessary and folding is too tight given the price. I’ll call to realize my equity, keep bluffs in his range, and fold to large river aggression unless I pair or complete the straight (or see a clear read to continue).

Board: Qh 2c 7c 8c 2d. My T♦9♦ has missed — no pair, no club flush, and my straight outs missed. Elon led flop and turn but checked the river, which is ambiguous: he could be pot‑controlling a vulnerable made hand (Qx, a club flush, or a deuce) or he could be giving up with a missed hand. Either way I have essentially no showdown value and poor blockers, and a river bluff will be called too often by a wide/aggressive opponent who barrels and also calls light. If Elon bets I’ll fold to meaningful river aggression; only a very tiny probe would merit a light call, but I won’t bloat the pot with air.

River board pairs the deuces (Qh 2c 7c 8c 2d), giving me top pair eights with a weak six kicker. The turn 8c completed the club flush draw, which Minni called my 800 bet after checking flop and turn, suggesting she may have a flush, strong two pair/set, or is floating/stationy. My top pair has significant showdown value against bluffs, missed draws (e.g., OESD like 45, 59), weaker pairs (77, Qx, low pairs), or even weak flushes trapping weakly. The paired board reduces straight outs and improves some hands but doesn't change flush nuts. Deep stacks (15860 effective ~158 BB), late in tournament (120/150), check induces fold equity or extracts calls from worse. But given consistent checks and prior calling station tendencies, a thin value bet ~2/3 pot (1800 into 2800) targets her range for value (Qx, 77, 92, worse 8x, Ax high), protects vs. rivers, maintains aggression to build stack, and position allows. Checking risks her having nothing and pot control unnecessary with value. Bet 1800 balances.

Pot is 4,600 and I must call 1,800, so I need roughly 28% equity to break even (1,800 / (4,600+1,800) ≈ 28%). My T♦9♦ missed the river — no pair and no club flush on Q♥ 2♣ 7♣ 8♣ 2♦ — so I have very little showdown value. That said Elon is a wide, aggressive BTN who barrels a lot and the river bet is relatively small (~39% of the pot), which often contains thin value and bluffs. Folding here would be too tight given the cheap price; raising OOP as a bluff with no pair on a three‑club, paired board is low EV and very risky. I'll take the cheap shot to realize my equity and call 1,800, prepared to fold to any meaningful raise/raise‑and‑shove (I won’t bloat the pot with air).

Heads-up on the button with Q♥9♥ — a playable, suited hand that plays well postflop and benefits from position. Opponent (Elon) defends wide from the big blind, so opening for value and fold equity is correct. I’ll raise to 300 total (3BB) — a standard, slightly larger than min size that wins many unraised pots preflop, builds a pot when I’m ahead of his calling range (we dominate many A/x and K/x and weaker Qx hands), and keeps postflop playable with deep stacks. If he 3-bets big I can fold or re-evaluate; but limp/call is too passive and surrenders initiative. Committing 300 total is the right exploitative, position-based play here.

Heads-up against Minni on the button who raised to 300. My Th4h is suited with straight and flush potential. In heads-up BB vs BTN, defend wide preflop. Pot odds are favorable: calling 200 more into 400 pot (roughly 2:1). Hand has good playability postflop with implied odds given deep stacks (~200BB). Folding too tight; 3-betting risky with speculative hand. Prefer to call and realize equity.

Flop board As Ah Js is extremely strong and dry, heavily favoring Minni's button raising range which likely includes Ax, AJs+, JJ+, KQs type hands that crush us. Th4h has zero equity: no pair, no flush draw (only backdoor hearts), no straight draw (T high with irrelevant 4). Pot 600, deep stacks, but spewing chips by betting out with air into a strong range is -EV. Check to induce her likely continuation bet and fold, preserving stack. If she checks behind (unlikely), we get a free card with backdoors. Folding pre was an option, but we called wide; now play disciplined postflop.

Flop: As Ah Js. I have Qh9h — no pair, only backdoor heart potential and very little showdown value. Elon defended wide preflop but then checked the flop; that check is often weakness (or a trap with an Ace). The board heavily hits his calling range (many Ax and Jx), so I don’t want to build a big pot with near-zero equity. In heads-up position I should apply pressure but keep the bet small to fold out Kx/Qx/weak broadways and to preserve stack vs hands that beat me. I will continuation-bet ~1/3 pot (200 chips). Plan: represent an Ace/J and take the pot down now; if he check-raises significantly I’ll fold and if he calls I’ll re-evaluate turn with pot control.

Facing a small continuation bet of 200 into 800 on a paired AsAhJs flop from Minni's button raising range, which hits her hard: any Ace, Jx, pocket pairs, sets, etc. Th4h is complete air—no pair, no flush draw (only backdoor heart), no straight draw. Pot odds 4:1 require ~20% equity, but we have <5% against her range. Deep stacks, but no implied odds justify calling with zero equity and poor playability. Preflop call was wide BB defense, but postflop discipline demands folding to avoid spewing chips. Check-fold as planned preserves stack for better spots.

Heads-up on the button/SB with KTo offsuit, a strong hand in this spot (top ~25% range). Deep stacks (~200BB), pot 150 after blinds. Standard play is to raise as first to act from SB vs BB; limping is suboptimal as it allows BB to check strong hands or realize equity cheaply. Minni defends wide based on history. Raising to 3x BB (300) builds the pot while maintaining fold equity. Avoids folding equity issues and positions me well post-flop.

Heads-up in the big blind with A♥ Q♣ against Elon's open to 300 (3x BB). AQo is a strong, predominantly ahead hand versus a wide SB opening range; it plays well both for value and as a blocker to some of Elon's premium combos. I want to seize the initiative, get fold equity, and isolate the opener rather than allowing cheap multi-street play out of position. A standard 3-bet sizing here keeps the pot manageable while exerting pressure, so I’ll raise to 900 total. Plan: if called, c-bet favorable A/Q flops, pot-control on dangerous boards; fold to a large 4-bet shove from a very narrow nuts-heavy range, but re-evaluate smaller 4-bets based on pot odds and reads.

Heads-up preflop on the button/SB with KTo offsuit after raising to 300 and facing a 3-bet to 900 from Minni in BB. Pot 1200, call 600 (pot odds ~2:1 direct, better implied). Deep stacks (~200BB effective). KTo has good playability in position postflop, decent equity (~35-40%) vs Minni's likely 3-bet range (tight value like QQ+, AK, plus some bluffs from history of aggression). Folding too tight heads-up; 4-bet risks with marginal blocker (K) and offsuit T, better to realize equity OOP for her but IP for us. History shows Minni 3-bets/raises wide, calls stations postflop. Call to play postflop with position, pot control, and stack off opportunities if we hit K/T or draw.

I 3-bet pre with A♥Q♣ and got called, so I still have initiative. The flop 2♣-4♦-6♠ is very dry and misses most of Elon's calling range — a textbook spot to continuation-bet. With the Ace-blocker and no strong draws on board I can get folds from Kx/Qx/weak Ax and charge small pairs. I’ll bet ~2/3 pot to maximize fold equity and value (1200 into 1800). Plan: if he folds we take it down; if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and proceed cautiously; if he raises big I’ll give credit to a set and likely fold.

Heads-up postflop on a dry 2c4d6s rainbow board after calling her 3-bet preflop with KTo offsuit. Pot 3000, facing a 1200 bet (2/3 pot) as the IP player. My hand is king-high with no pair, no straight draw (T gap too big for 246), weak backdoor spade draw. Preflop call was to realize equity IP with playability vs her wide 3-bet range, but this board hits few of our outs (only running K/T) and favors her polarized range (sets like 22/44/66, straight draws like 35/53, or strong Ax/Kx). History shows Minni c-bets aggressively OOP but value-heavy after 3-betting; king-high has ~30% equity vs her range but poor implied odds and reverse implied if she has Ax. Raising as bluff risks her 4-bet shove with monsters or floats; flat commits too deep OOP for her next streets without improving. Optimal fold preserves stack for better spots in late tournament with deep effective stacks (~180BB).

Heads-up on the button (I’m SB) with K♠Q♥ — a strong, standard opening hand. Limping/calling is too passive; I want initiative and position post‑flop. Min-raise to 200 is too small and gives Elon good odds to play back; I’ll raise to 300 (≈3x the BB) to get value from worse hands and to apply pressure. Plan: if called, c-bet most K/Q/fair flops and exercise pot control on A‑high boards; against a large 3‑bet I’ll re-evaluate (often folding KQo to heavy 3‑bets, calling small 3‑bets depending on sizing).

Heads-up play late in the tournament with deep stacks (~190BB effective). Minni raises to 3BB from the button, which is a standard open with a very wide range including all pairs, broadway, suited aces, suited connectors, and many offsuit broadways. Ts8h is a playable defending hand in the BB vs button: decent equity (~42% vs a 40% button range), straight potential with T8, and some blocker value with the Ten. Pot odds are excellent: calling 200 into 400 (pot becomes 600) requires only ~25% equity, and we have much more. Folding is too tight heads-up; 3-betting is possible but this hand prefers multiway postflop potential or realizing equity by calling. We'll call, then play postflop aggressively on favorable boards (e.g., check-raise draws, floats) and control pot OOP. Goal is to accumulate chips over remaining 27 hands.

Heads-up on the flop OOP with 19k effective stacks (~190BB). Board Js7c6d is coordinated with straight draws (e.g., T9,98,54) and pair+draw combos. Ts8h gives an open-ended straight draw (9 outs, ~32% equity if called), plus backdoor flush and straight potential. Minni's button range is wide (40-50%: broadway, pairs, suited connectors) and hits this board decently (Jx, 76,77,66, T9,98). As OOP first to act, betting risks: gets raised by better (sets, two-pair, Jx), folds worse to c-bet range, or allows free cards. Checking is optimal: induces bluffs/c-bets from Ax,Kx,Qx,misses (wide); allows check-raise semi-bluff/value on wide range; realizes equity cheaply if checked behind; controls pot OOP. Plan: check, call small bets (~300-600) for odds, check-raise bigger bets (~1000+) as semi-bluff (folds J-high, overpairs sometimes), fold to stubborn 4-bets. Accumulates EV long-term vs aggressive Minni.

Heads‑up on the button with K♠Q♥ on Js‑7c‑6d: I have two clean overcards and position on a relatively dry board (no flush, only a few straight draws like 8‑9/9‑10). Elon checked — this is an ideal spot to c‑bet to take down the pot and to get value/fold equity against his wide calling range from the BB. I’ll size to about 0.55–0.65 pot to charge any straight draws and to fold out small pairs/underpairs while getting called by worse Jx/Tx/Kx/Qx sometimes; if he flats I’ll evaluate turn, if he jams/raises huge I’ll fold KQo. Betting 350 into the 600 pot balances fold equity and protection.

Heads-up deep-stacked (~190BB) on a draw-heavy flop Js7c6d. Ts8h gives a clean open-ended straight draw (8 outs: 4x5, 4x9 for nuts or near-nuts straights), ~32% equity baseline, plus T overcard and backdoor potential. OOP after check vs Minni's smallish 350 c-bet into 950 (~37% pot), which mixes value (Jx, 76/66/77 sets, two-pair draws) with bluffs (Ax/Kx/Qx, gutshots like T9/98, air). Her button range hits medium but vulnerable. Calling offers odds (350/1300 ~27%) and playability, but OOP bloating pot risks tough turns. Check-raising semi-bluffs optimally: exploits fold equity vs overcards/A-high/J-high (~40-50% her range folds), builds pot with equity when called, disguises as sets/Jx, and denies equity. Size to ~3x (1050-1100) balances vs minraise/call/3bet ranges. Vs history of Minni's aggression (frequent PF raises, flop bets, but folds to pressure like H3,H44,H51), high EV to raise and take control. Maximizes stack growth late tournament.

I opened to 300 and c‑bet 350 on Js‑7c‑6d; Elon check‑raised to 1100 so I must call 750 (min reraise to 1850). The pot is 2050; calling 750 requires ~26.8% equity (750/(2050+750)). K♠Q♥ is just two overcards with ~24% or less equity to improve to a winning hand by river against a check‑raising range that will be heavy in Jx, 7x/6x and overpairs. Raising as a bluff here with deep stacks is reckless — it bloats the pot and opens me to a shove/call with many hands that dominate me. Elon’s line looks value‑oriented; fold and preserve my stack for a better spot.

Heads-up on the button/SB with Qc4d, a marginal but playable hand. In heads-up play, the BTN/SB raising range is very wide (50%+ of hands) due to position and fold equity against the BB. Q4o has a queen blocker, decent high card value, and position post-flop allows controlling the pot. Minni calls/raises frequently but folds enough pre-flop to make this profitable. Raising standard 3x BB seizes initiative, builds pot with position. Deep stacks (200BB), no need to limp which invites trouble. Fold too tight, call passive.

Heads‑up vs Elon; I'm BB with TcJs and he opened to 300 (pot 400, I need 200). In heads‑up my calling range should be wide, but TcJs is strong enough to 3‑bet for value and fold equity against his wide open. Folding is too tight; just calling keeps me out of position postflop against a player who c‑bets/pressures a lot. I want to isolate and take initiative, so I’ll make a standard 3x raise to 900. That sizing gives good fold equity vs weaker holdings (small pairs, suited connectors, many broadways) while extracting value from worse Ax/Kx and wide calling ranges. If Elon 4‑bets I can reevaluate (hand plays okay vs a 4‑bet but I won’t be auto‑committed with deep stacks). Action: raise to 900.

Heads-up pre-flop on the button facing a 3-bet to 900 after my open to 300 (already 300 in, call 600 more). Pot 1200 offers excellent odds: 600 to win 1800 (~25% equity needed). Qc4d has a strong queen blocker (blocks QQ, AQ), decent high-card strength, and good post-flop playability in position with deep stacks (~190BB effective). Minni's BB 3-bet range is polarized and wide heads-up (premiums like JJ+, AKo/AQs+, plus suited Ax, broadway bluffs), where Q4o realizes 30-40% equity depending on exact range, plus strong implied odds OOP for her but position for me. Folding too tight allows her to 3-bet wide profitably; calling keeps pot small (~1800), realizes equity, and maintains initiative potential. 4-betting risks too much with marginal hand vs possible monsters; prefer to see flop cheap in position. Consistent with wide BTN defense strategy to maximize EV long-term.

I was the 3-bettor preflop and Elon's call is wide in heads‑up. The flop 5c-6s-2h is very dry and heavily favors the preflop aggressor's range. I hold TcJs — two overcards and a backdoor club — so I have some showdown/backdoor equity but no made hand. This is a textbook spot to continuation‑bet to take the initiative and fold out unpaired overcards and many marginal hands (weak Ax, Kx, small connectors) while keeping the pot controllable. Pot = 1800; I’ll make a small c‑bet (~1/3 pot = 600) to maximize fold equity and preserve stack for later streets. If Elon calls I can barre lturn selectively; if he raises big I can re‑evaluate (likely fold to heavy aggression with only overcards).

Heads-up in position on a dry 5c6s2h flop after calling a 3-bet pre-flop with Qc4d, a queen-high hand with good blockers (blocks QQ/AQ). Pot is 1800 before Minni's 600 donk bet (1/3 pot sizing), offering pot odds of 2400:600 or 4:1, requiring only ~17% equity to call profitably. Q4o blocks strong value hands in her polarized BB 3-bet range (JJ+, AK, suited broadways/Ax for value; bluffs like A5s-K9s, suited connectors/wheel plays), and queen-high has showdown value against many of her bluff-heavy combos she might donk to protect or charge draws. No strong draws on board (only gutshots like 34/47), rainbow texture favors my high-card strength. Position allows controlling pot post-flop, realizing equity effectively with deep stacks (~190BB). Raising risks bloating pot OOP for her with marginal equity (~30-35% vs her range) and stacks her monsters; folding is too tight given wide defense strategy and history of Minni's aggression/stickiness in pots (she calls large bets frequently in prior hands). Calling keeps pot manageable (~3000), invites bluffs on later streets, sets up floats/raises if she checks turn, and consistent with exploiting her wide 3-bet/donk tendencies long-term to maximize EV over remaining 26 hands.

I was the preflop 3‑bettor and c‑bet the dry 5c-6s-2h flop; Elon called. The turn Ks is a bad card for my holding (Tc Js) — it hits a large portion of his call range (Kx and many Ax/pocket pairs) and removes much of the fold equity I had on the flop. I have no pair and very little meaningful equity now, so barreling here OOP risks building a pot I’m likely behind in and opens me up to a check‑raise I should fold to. With deep stacks and a small current pot (3,000) it’s better to control the pot size and see a free river. Plan: check and call small turn bets to realize equity, fold to large aggression, and if he checks back re‑evaluate on the river (can bluff or check for showdown depending on the river). Action: check to keep the pot manageable and avoid tough decisions without a made hand.

Heads-up on button with Qc4d after calling preflop 3-bet and flop donk into 3000 pot on 5c6s2hKs board. Minni checks turn, giving me initiative in position with ~184BB effective stacks and 26 hands left. Qc blocks QQ/AQ (key value in her polarized BB 3-bet/donk range: premiums JJ+, AKo, suited broadways/Ax value; bluffs suited Ax/connectors/wheel Ax). Queen-high has showdown value vs many bluffs/protection bets she donked flop with (weak pairs 77-TT crushed by K, missed draws like 34/A3/47 gutshots, spade draws now live post-Ks but thin). Board low+Ks favors my blocker-heavy queen overpair blocks. Her flop donk then turn check suggests weakness (missed K improvement, trapping unlikely given history of aggression then folding to turns/rivers). Deep stacks: betting exploits her stickiness (frequent calls in history) for thin value vs worse (Jx/Tx/87 SDV, weaker Ax) while charging draws (spades, gutshots) and denying equity. Size 2/3 pot (1800 into 3000, pot becomes ~6600) balances value/protection, small enough vs monsters, large for folds. Checking realizes equity but misses fold equity (~35-45% equity calling station range) and lets her bluff river free; betting +EV long-term maximizing stack variance tolerance. Consistent wide postflop strategy punishing her wide 3-bet/donk.

I will call 1800. Elon’s 1800 into a 4800 pot is a relatively small turn bet (pot after a call would be 6600) so calling requires ~27% equity (1800/6600). I was the preflop 3‑bettor and c‑bet the dry flop; the King on the turn heavily improved a large portion of Elon’s calling range (Kx, many Ax, pocket pairs). My TcJs has no pair now and very little fold equity OOP, so a raise here would be thin and likely to get called or 4‑bett by hands that dominate me. Calling keeps the pot under control, realizes my showdown equity, and lets me see the river for a cheaper price. Plan: call the turn, fold to a large cold 4‑bet/raise, and on the river re‑evaluate — fold to big river aggression unless I pair or pick up a clear bluffing spot.

I paired my Ten on the river (Tc + Th) giving me a clean one‑pair showdown hand with a Jack kicker. Elon has called my 3‑bet and my flop c‑bet, then fired a sizable turn bet — that line contains Kx, some medium pocket pairs and a mix of weaker/creative calls. Checking risks him firing another sizable river bet (putting me to a tough fold), while a small value bet charges worse single‑pair hands and many busted overcards that will call. I’ll bet small to extract thin value and deny a free bluff: ~1/3 pot is large enough to get value but small enough to keep worse hands in. Bet 2200; if Elon raises big I’ll assume he has Kx/strong value and fold.

Heads-up on button with Qc4d (queen-high) facing a 1/3 pot river bet of 2200 into 6600 (pot odds ~4:1, needing ~20% equity to call profitably). Board 5c6s2hKsTh is paired-low with K/T overcards; possible straights (e.g., 34 for 23456, 47/78 gutshots earlier) and flush draws (spades from turn). Pre-flop Minni's BB 3-bet range polarized (JJ+, AK/AQs+, bluffs like suited Ax/connectors); flop donk suggests value (pairs/sets) or bluffs/draws; turn check-call after my 1800 bet indicates medium strength (likely hit K, two-pair potential like K5/K6/K2, 65, 76 str, or float with position denial); river lead (after check-call turn) strongly favors value (KT, KT2o, K9+, trips/set with K/T, completed str 34) over pure bluff (missed spade draw unlikely to lead small after calling turn, as it risks getting check-raised). Qc blocks QQ/AQ (not huge here) but queen-high loses to all pairs (T+, 76 str beats), Kx dominant in range. SDV only vs pure air (AJ+/missed draws ~10-15% of range post-turn call), equity <20% (closer to 10-15%). Deep stacks (~160BB), ICM irrelevant late with 26 hands left; folding preserves stack to exploit better spots vs Minni's sticky tendencies without spewing ~13% stack on marginal. Consistent with disciplined range-based play over tournament.

Heads-up on the button (also SB) with J♥9♣ — a playable, positionally-advantaged hand. Calling 50 (limping) gives up initiative; I want to open and take the lead. A standard ~3x BB open to 300 achieves fold equity, isolates the BB, and keeps post‑flop playability with deep stacks. If Elon folds I scoop the 150 pot; if he calls I’ll use position to c‑bet favorable, control pot size on coordinated/high boards, and realize equity. If he 3‑bets large I’ll fold this marginal hand; versus a small 3‑bet I may call and play in position. Total commitment: 300 (I’ve posted 50, so I’ll add 250).

75o is a complete trash hand with no straight potential, no suitedness, and poor playability OOP. Minni's button raise is a standard steal attempt with a wide range, but calling or 3-betting has no value. Pot odds are decent (200 to call into 400), but postflop I'll be OOP with a hand that rarely improves and has no bluff value. Preserve stack for better spots with 26 hands left. Standard fold.

Heads-up play on the button/SB position pre-flop with J2o. This is a marginal but playable hand in heads-up No-Limit Hold'em, as opening ranges from the button are very wide (typically 50-70% of hands) to apply pressure and steal the dead money in the pot. Minni defends wide based on hand history but folds to aggression often enough post-flop. Raising standard 3x the BB (300 total) provides fold equity on the 150 pot while keeping pot small if called. Folding is too tight this deep in the tournament with small blinds relative to stacks (16k effective). Limping is suboptimal as it invites action OOP and denies fold equity. Post-flop, even if called, J2o has some straight potential and live Js blocker.

Heads-up vs button open to 300 and I’m in the big blind with J♦T♥. Stacks are deep (≈234/162 bb in chips), pot is 400 and I must call 200 more — break-even equity is 200/(400+200)=33.3%. JTo has reasonable equity and good playability heads-up against a wide button opening range, and deep stacks give strong implied odds to realize equity. I’m out of position postflop, so I’d rather keep the pot controlled and see a flop than iso-3bet with a marginal hand; a small 3-bet won’t gain much fold equity against an aggressive opener and only bloats a multi-street pot. Plan: call, see flop, and proceed based on texture (continue on favorable broadway/heart-heavy flops, pot-control or fold on dry boards to heavy aggression).

Check. I'm out of position with J♦T♥ on 6♣-4♠-5♠: no pair, no spade backdoor and only two live overcards. The flop is low, connected and two-spade which hits a big portion of the button’s wide opening range (small pairs, spade draws, 3/7-style combinations). Leading here has poor fold equity and just bloats a multi-street pot OOP with a marginal hand. With deep stacks I want to control the pot size and realize equity rather than build a big pot. Plan: check and see what Elon does. If he bets a small/medium probe (roughly ≤~1/3–1/2 pot) I’ll call to see a turn and re-evaluate; fold to heavy pressure (large pot-sized/overbets) unless I improve. If checked through I’ll take a free card on the turn and continue only with improvement or a clear fold vs aggression.

Flop completely misses my J2o: jack-high with no pair, no flush draw, no straight draw (though backdoor spade with Js). Board 6c-4s-5s is highly drawy: two spades (flush draw), wheel straight draws (3x), open-enders (3-7, 7x). Minni checked from BB, suggesting possible weakness, but her history shows she calls stations post-flop and defends wide preflop. As preflop raiser with wide button range, c-betting has merit for fold equity, but this isn't a great bluff spot OOP for her—my range hits some (pairs, sets, overpairs, combo draws). In position with deep stacks (16k eff., 600 pot), checking realizes equity for free, controls pot size, and sets up turns where I can bluff missed draws or value thin. Betting air risks variance without great equity (~25-35% vs her calling range) or fold equity given board favors her range more. Goal: maximize stack long-term; check is disciplined GTO-ish play here.

I check. Reasoning: heads‑up vs the button’s wide open, I called preflop to keep the pot under control and we both checked the flop. The turn (5♥) pairs the board and I still have no pair, no nut draw, and mediocre showdown value with J♦T♥. Elon checking the flop strongly narrows his line toward missed overcards, small pairs, or slowplays — many of which will continue vs a small bet and some will raise when OOP. With deep effective stacks and no fold‑equity as the out‑of‑position player, betting here bloats a multi‑street pot with a marginal hand and hands that will call me down. Checking preserves stack, allows me to realize equity on the river for free, and keeps future options (call a small river probe, fold to large pressure, or value/raise if I improve). Plan: check and re‑evaluate on the river based on Elon’s action and the card.

Turn board pairs the 5h, creating possible trips or two pair for villain's BB calling range (5x, 45, etc.). My J2o remains complete air: jack-high, no pair, no flush draw, minimal backdoor equity (spade/club gutshot unlikely). Preflop raiser checked flop (per previous reasoning to realize equity), villain checks again OOP, indicating possible weakness but also consistent with trapping medium strength or draws. Villain's history shows frequent light calls post-flop and on rivers (e.g., Hands 7,9,33,51+), suggesting station tendencies who defends wide and pays off bluffs poorly. Pot 600 with 16k effective stacks, betting now commits unnecessary variance with ~0 equity and low fold equity vs her range. Checking in position controls pot, sees free river (24 hands left, preserve stack for better spots), allows bluffing missed draws later or showdown if she is weak. Disciplined check to maximize long-term EV vs exploitable opponent.

Check. Both players checked the flop and turn and I have no pair or meaningful draw on a paired 6c-4s-5s-5h-2h board with J♦T♥. Betting as OOP with zero showdown value will either get called by any one-pair hands (losing value) or fold only a small fraction of Elon's checking range, so bluffing here is thin. With deep stacks I prefer pot control and a free showdown — I’ll check and fold to a sizable bet on the river (or call a very small probe if the price and line make it reasonable).

River 2h pairs my 2c, giving me bottom pair (deuces) with J kicker on a board of 6c-4s-5s-5h-2h. Possible opponent holdings: trips 5s, two-pair combos (45s, 64s, 52s etc.), wheel straights (A3, 3x), higher pairs that improved, or bluffs/missed draws (spade flush draws, heart draws post-turn, gutshots). Preflop caller from BB with checks all streets OOP suggests medium strength, air, or traps, but history shows Minni stations light calls frequently (e.g., Hands 9,33,51,70,80+). As IP preflop raiser, my range is perceived wide including value (pairs, sets, draws) and bluffs. Bottom pair has showdown value vs her wide defending range (Ax, Kx, Qx, busted draws ~30-40% equity). Pot 600, SPR low late tournament (24 hands left, effective 16k), bet half-pot 400 extracts value from worse (worse pairs, high card) while denying equity to draws and keeping fold equity vs monsters. Checking risks free showdown but misses thin value vs calling station. Betting exploits her tendencies, builds stack without overcommitting.

Elon opened button to 300, we called OOP with JdTh. Flop and turn checked through, and Elon fires a small river bet of 400 into a 1000 pot. Pot odds: we must call 400 to win a pot of 1400 total after our call, so break‑even equity ≈ 400/(1000+400) = 28.6%. Given Elon’s wide HU opening range and the passive two‑street line, his river bet is consistent with a mixed range of thin value hands (small pairs like 6x/4x/2x or thin one‑pair bets) and missed draws/overcards turned into a small probe/bluff. Our JTo has no showdown winner vs made pairs but actually has decent chop/win equity vs missed overcards and many bluffs; we also have a very small chunk of the stacks at risk relative to stack depths. Raising as a bluff OOP with no pair or strong blockers is thin and likely to get called or raised by better, so folding would give up >28.6% equity for just 400 chips. Therefore the +EV line is to call the 400 and see showdown.

Heads-up on the button with Q9o — a hand I want to be raising with. I have positional advantage and the stacks are deep, so a standard open to the min (to 200 total) gains initiative, builds the pot with a hand that has reasonable equity on many flops, and applies pressure to the BB. If Elon 3-bets large I will fold (or re-evaluate if it’s a small sizing); if called I’ll c-bet favorable boards (Q/T/9 or dry textures) and play cautiously on wet boards. The raise is small relative to the stacks and preserves flexibility postflop.

Heads-up in BB with AKo facing a standard SB min-raise to 200. AKo is a premium hand at the top of my range, with great equity against Minni's likely wide SB raising range, which includes many weaker aces, kings, suited connectors, and bluffs based on hand history showing frequent aggression. OOP, but deep stacks (17k effective). Calling gives up initiative and plays OOP postflop, which is suboptimal. 3-betting builds the pot for value, applies pressure, and denies equity to weaker hands. Sizing to 800 (4x her raise) is standard vs minraise, balances value and bluff frequency. Favorable pot odds post-3-bet if called. Fold is never correct.

Elon 3-bet to 800 after my 200 open. Heads‑up on the button with Q9o, I have position and decent post‑flop playability, so I’d rather keep the pot controllable and realize equity than fold or thinly 4‑bet. The 3‑bet sizing is standard but not so large that calling is irresponsible given deep stacks — a 4‑bet to 1400 here is too thin with Q9o and will bloat the pot or force a tough fold to a 5‑bet. Plan: call to use position postflop, c‑bet for value on favorable dry Q/T/9 boards, check and pot‑control on wet/overcard boards, and fold to large, polarized aggression if I miss.

Flop Ts3sAc gives me top pair top kicker (AhKc) on a board that connects well with my 3-bet range. As preflop aggressor OOP, c-betting for value and protection against her calling range, which likely includes worse Ax (like A-rag suited), flush draws (spade blockers), straight draws (KQ/JT suited), and some two-pair/set value. Spade draw is live, so betting builds pot when ahead and charges draws. Pot 1600, betting 1000 (~60% pot) is balanced size: denies equity to draws, gets value from worse, and leaves room for turns/rivers. Stacks deep (16k eff), but hand strong enough to bet without overcommitting. Checking risks giving free card to draws or letting her rep AK/AQ/sets OOP. History shows Minni calls stations with draws/big pots.

I called the 3‑bet preflop to use position, but this flop (Ts 3s Ac) is terrible for Q9s. Elon 3‑bet preflop and now bets 1,000 into a 2,600 pot — calling 1,000 would require about 1,000 / (2,600+1,000) ≈ 27.8% equity to break even. I have no pair, only a very weak runner‑runner spade backdoor and a few cards (Q or 9) that rarely get me to >27% vs a 3‑bettor’s continuing range (lots of Ax, TT+, AK, etc.). Raising here would be a thin bluff that bloats the pot out of position. Given my actual equity is well below the required threshold and Elon’s line is value‑heavy, folding is the correct, lowest‑variance choice to preserve my stack for better spots.

Heads-up on the button/SB with deep stacks (180BB+). J6o is a strong enough hand in this spot for a standard open-raise to steal the blinds and build the pot in position. Limping is suboptimal as it invites a check-raise; folding is too tight given the wide SB opening range (50-70% of hands). Minni has been aggressive but folds to continuation bets frequently post-flop based on history. Raising to 3x (300 total) is a standard size to apply pressure while keeping pot manageable.

Elon (button / small blind) opened to 300. I have A♣4♣ — a suited Ace that blocks many stronger A combos and has decent postflop playability heads-up. Stacks are very deep, but I’ll be out of position on the flop (I act first), and Elon has shown aggressive tendencies, so I don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal hand by making a small 3-bet. I’ll defend by calling the extra 200 to see a flop (total committed 300) and then evaluate flop texture (look to continue on A/club flops or proceed cautiously if I miss).

I have A♣4♣ on Jc-6c-8h: the Ac gives me the nut-flush draw (9 clubs left) and an Ace blocker to many strong A combos. I’m out of position against an aggressive preflop raiser so I don’t want to bloat the pot with a lead. Checking keeps the pot under control and preserves flexibility: if Elon c-bets I can check‑raise as a semi-bluff to leverage fold equity, or call larger bets to realize my ~35%+ equity (flush outs plus 3 A outs) and reevaluate on the turn. If he checks back I can take the initiative on the turn. Overall the best immediate line is to check and let him show his hand/size.

Preflop: Raised J6o from SB/button as a standard play in heads-up deepstacked spot for initiative and pot building. Postflop: Board 6c-Jc-8h hits my hand perfectly for top two pair (Js over Jc, 6d over 6c), which is very strong here. Minni's check suggests weakness or a draw, but she calls pre frequently. Coordinated board with club flush draw and straight draws (e.g., 57, 79, 9T, 45), so need to bet for value from worse pairs/draws and protection against draws. Deep stacks allow pot control but value betting maximizes EV. Half-pot c-bet sizes well: builds pot, denies equity to draws, induces calls from weaker hands. Avoid check-back as it gives free cards to improve. Bet 400 (2/3 pot effectively, standard sizing).

I have A♣4♣ on J♣6♣8♥ — the nut-club flush draw plus an Ace blocker. That’s 9 club outs + 3 non‑club Aces = 12 nominal outs; roughly ~45% to improve by the river. Elon’s bet is 400 into a 1000 pot: calling 400 would win ~1800, so I’m getting ~22% pot‑odds. My equity comfortably exceeds the price, so a call is +EV. Elon is aggressive and his small c‑bet represents a wide range (Jx, single clubs, air), so I’ll call to realize my equity, keep the pot controlled out of position, and re‑evaluate on the turn — if I hit a club or an Ace I can start extracting value; if I miss I’ll proceed cautiously versus further aggression.

Heads-up, out of position with A♣4♣ on J♣-6♣-8♥-5♦ — I have the nut-club flush draw plus an Ace blocker. Remaining outs = 9 clubs + 3 non-club Aces = 12 outs, so turn→river equity ≈ 12/46 ≈ 26.1%. Elon bet 400 into the ~1000 pot; calling 400 would make the final pot ~1800, so required equity = 400/1800 ≈ 22.2%. My equity (≈26%) > required equity, so a call is +EV. I prefer to call rather than raise: I’m OOP, his sizing is small (wide range), and calling keeps the pot controlled while preserving the ability to extract value or fold on the river depending on the card and his action. Plan: call the 400, and on the river value-bet when I hit (flush or Ace) or fold to large aggression if I miss and he barrels hard.

Turn board 6c-Jc-8h-5d completes some straight draws (e.g., 4-7, 7-9) and adds a diamond flush draw possibility, but no flush completes yet. Top two pair (Js over Jc, 6d over 6c) remains very strong, ahead of most of Minni's calling range from flop (top pair like Jx, mid pairs 88/66 unlikely, draws like clubs or 79s). Her check-call flop then check suggests medium strength or draw; deep stacks (170BB+ effective) favor continued thin value betting to extract from weaker pairs/draws while protecting against completing draws. Checking risks giving free cards to clubs/7/9/4/A/T river cards that improve her or kill action. Bet 2/3 pot (~900) builds pot for river value, denies equity, standard sizing vs. calling station tendencies observed in history (frequent calls down light but folds to heavy aggression sometimes). Avoids overbetting to prevent folds or min-raises from sets.

I have A♣4♣: the nut club draw plus an Ace blocker (9 club outs + 3 non‑club Aces = 12 outs → turn→river ≈ 26%). The pot is 2300 and Elon’s turn bet is 900. A simple call would require ~28.1% equity (900/(2300+900)) so pure calling is slightly -EV vs this sizing. Raising to the minimum (to 1800 total) gives me fold equity and is a strong semi‑bluff: if he folds I scoop the 2300 pot; if he calls we go to a 5000 pot and my equity is ~26% (EV when called ≈ 0.26*5000 − 1800 = −500). Solving shows the raise is +EV if he folds at least ≈18% to my raise (500/2800 ≈ 17.9%). Given Elon’s wide c‑betting/barrel tendencies on these boards, I expect him to fold at least that often. Raising also protects my draw, applies pressure to Jx and small single‑club hands, and builds a pot I can win big when I hit. Plan: raise to the min (1800 total). If he folds I collect the pot; if he calls I still have ~26% to improve and will play the river based on the card and his action; if he 3‑bets big I’ll reassess/fold rather than committing without a made hand.

Top two pair Js6d (J over Jc, 6 over 6c) remains very strong on 6c-Jc-8h-5d turn board. Minni's check-call flop fits wide (Jx, pairs, draws like clubs/OESD 79/57); her check-raise turn after my value bet narrows to ~10-15% range: nuts/strong value (sets 55/66/JJ/88 ~12 combos, J8 two pair ~4, JcX flush draw ~8), straights (47xx ~12 combos), combo draws/semi-bluffs (cluby overcards, 79 clubs etc. ~20). Equity ~65% vs this (beats Jx/draws/semi-bluffs, loses to sets/J8/47). Blocks Js6d from her range slightly. Deep effective stacks (~160BB), calling 900 into 4100 pot offers 4.55:1 direct + massive implied odds vs her continuing range (draws pay off rivers, marginal Jx call down). Raising folds out worse (draws/Jx pay more calling rivers), risks her jamming monsters (forced thinner call). Folding too tight heads-up deep. Call to realize equity, control pot size; river OOP: value bet if she checks (her range polarized), fold to huge jam/scary card, call/raise smaller. Minni history: calls stations light but check-raises turn with equity/value, maximizes EV long-term stack growth.

I hold A♣4♣; the flush draw missed and the river brings 4♠ so I now have a weak pair of 4s with an Ace blocker. Pot is 5,000 and Elon checked to me after calling my turn raise — his line (preflop raise, flop c-bet, call of my turn semi-bluff, then check) is consistent with a wide but often-medium range: Jx/8x/6x, some pocket pairs, missed connectors, and some Ace/K/Q high floats. My pair is too weak to thin-value-bet big, but it has thin showdown value vs worse hands (A‑high/K‑high/other floats) and can get value from his missed hands. I prefer a small, low-risk value stab to charge those worse hands and fold out pure air; if he raises substantially I’ll fold (I don’t want to inflate the pot with a weak pair). Betting ~30% pot keeps the price small, extracts thin value, and preserves fold equity. Action: small value bet of 1,500 chips.

River 4s completes 4-5-6-7-8 straights for 7x hands (e.g., 47s,57s,67s,77,set 77), possible but her turn check-raise range already included value like sets (66/88/JJ), J8 two pair, strong Jx clubs. Bluffs/missed draws (clubs like QcTc/KcAc, 79s combo draws) continue polarized with lead as bluff or thin value. My top two Js6d (blocks Js,6d) crushes Jx (JT,J9,J7,J5?), pairs, missed draws (~60-70% equity vs range); loses only ~30-40% to monsters (sets, J8,7x). Pot odds 6500:1500=4.33:1 direct + implied (her history calls light OOP, but deep stacks allow river jam if ahead). Folding too tight heads-up (strong SDV); raising risks folding out worse/isolating better, pot-committing deep (~10BB bet). Call realizes equity, controls pot, exploits her calling station tendencies (frequent light calls in history like H9,33,H33-H74). Maximizes EV/stack growth vs exploitable aggro range.

Heads-up on the button with 6♠6♥ and deep stacks (~180bb). I want to take the initiative and use position postflop, while extracting value from worse hands and denying Elon a free flop. A standard 3x BB open (raise to 300 total) achieves good fold equity and keeps the pot playable if called. If Elon 3-bets big I'll re-evaluate (call to see a flop given the depth, fold to extreme sizing), but open-raising is the +EV line here rather than limping or min-raising.

KQs suited is a premium hand heads-up, especially OOP in the BB facing a standard button/SB raise to 3BB. Minni is aggressive and raises frequently from late position based on hand history, but KQs has excellent equity against her calling range (dominates many Ax, suited broadways, and plays well postflop). Calling realizes equity but gives her position; 3-betting takes initiative, builds the pot for value, and denies her equity with worse hands. With deep stacks (219BB effective), sizing to 900 (3x her raise) is standard and balanced. Folding is -EV. Late in tournament (hand 129/150), but this spot is too strong to pass up for stack maximization.

Call to 900 total (600 more). With 6♥6♠ heads-up and very deep stacks, flat-calling the 3‑bet is the +EV line: I keep position, preserve my implied odds for set-mining, and avoid inflating the pot out of position with a marginal hand. Elon’s 3‑bet range is likely fairly wide (big pairs, broadways, Axs, suited connectors), so 66 has reasonable equity and only needs ~33% to break even on the call — plus I can win a large pot when I hit a set. A small 4‑bet here (to 1500) would often be called or might commit me to difficult postflop decisions against stronger holdings, and a large 4‑bet shove would be overleveraging a medium pair. Plan: call, use positional advantage postflop, fold to extreme aggression on bad boards, and extract value when I connect.

Heads-up on the flop with KQo suited (hearts), facing Minni who called my pre-flop 3-bet. The board 9h Jh Ts hits me perfectly with the nut flush draw (9 heart outs) and an open-ended straight draw to Broadway (3 Q outs + 4 8 outs, some also improving flush draw), giving me a monster combo draw (~30-40% equity vs. her 3-bet calling range of pairs, broadways, suited aces). Pot is 1800, I'm OOP first to act, deep stacks (~200BB effective). Checking risks giving free cards to her equity (e.g., straight draws like 78, AQ; sets; two-pair) or allowing bluffs/checks. Betting semi-bluffs for value against worse draws/pairs (e.g., AhXh, J10, smaller pairs), builds pot with massive equity, charges her draws, and denies equity. Sizing to ~2/3 pot (1200) balances value/protection/fold equity, standard in deep HU spots. Late tournament but stack maximization demands aggressive play with this strength.

Call 1200. I called the 3‑bet preflop to set‑mine and keep positional advantage; this flop is scary but the bet is small (1200 into ~3000), giving ~28.6% break‑even pot odds. 66 has reasonable equity vs Elon’s 3‑bet/c‑bet range and huge implied odds if I hit a set. Raising here is thin and will often put me in bloated pots dominated by Broadway hands; folding is too nit‑picky given the price and depth. Plan: call, use position to control the pot and re‑evaluate on the turn — proceed aggressively only if I improve or board becomes very favorable, and fold/defend cautiously to heavy turn/river aggression on coordinated/overcard runs.

Turn card 8d completes the nut straight (Q-J-T-9-8) with KQh, plus I retain the nut flush draw (9 heart outs) for massive redraw equity (~50%+ equity overall vs. her range). Board is very coordinated (now 8-9-T-J, many draws completed like 76 straight, flush draws possible), pot 4200, OOP first to act, deep effective stacks (~15k). Her flop call of my 1200 c-bet into 1800 on J-T-9hh board (after calling my 3bet pre) polarizes to strong made hands (JJ+/TT/99 sets, JT/J9/T9 two-pair, maybe Qx/Kx straight already, Ax draws) or draws (78, flush draws). Betting extracts max value from worse made hands (smaller two-pairs, top pair, inferior straights like 76), charges draws, builds pot for river shove potential if flush hits or as value. Checking risks free card to her villainous draws or induces bluff-raise I can check-raise, but denies value from calling range and OOP check risks her checking back monsters. Sizing ~2/3 pot (2800) balances value vs. her calling range, continues my aggressive line, standard in deep HU spot. Late tournament ICM minimal heads-up deep; stack max demands value extraction.

I called preflop and on the flop to set‑mine, but the turn 8♢ changes the texture dramatically — Qx (now) and 7x combos make straights and KQ/other broadway hands that were in Elon's 3‑betting range are very believable. Elon’s line (3‑bet pre, c‑bet, turn raise) is value‑heavy (Jx/T x, two‑pair, straights) and his turn sizing (2,800 into a 7,000 pot) gives me about 2800/(7000+2800) ≈ 28.6% break‑even equity to call. I have only ~4.3% to hit a set on the river and far less showdown equity versus his continuing range, so a call is -EV. A raise would be a thin bluff with poor blockers and a high chance of being called or shipped over. Fold and preserve the stack for better spots.

Heads-up pre-flop on the button/SB with A9s (suited Ace), a premium hand in this spot. Excellent position and playability. Minni has limped or checked BB (pot 150, to call 50), standard aggressive raise to 3x BB size (300 total). Builds pot with strong equity, folds out worse, and continues aggression post-flop if called. Consistent with my prior raising tendencies (frequent 300 raises). Avoid limping weak; maximize value and fold equity.

Heads-up vs Elon's button open to 300. I have J♦5♦ — marginal but suited and perfectly defendable in the big blind. Calling costs 200 more (300 total including my blind) into a 400 pot, giving ~2:1 immediate pot odds and excellent implied odds with both stacks deep (~150+ BB). Elon is aggressive and barrels often, so a flat call keeps the pot manageable and lets me realize equity: I'll continue with diamonds, a J/5 pair, two-pair/sets, or strong connected boards; on dry, overcard-heavy flops I'll fold to significant pressure. A small 3-bet bluff is possible, but given his postflop aggression and likelihood to 4-bet/continue big, I prefer to call and play the flop.

Flop: 5c 5s Jc — I hold Jd5d, which is a full house (fives full of jacks). This is an almost-nut hand here; the only real hand that beats me is pocket JJs, and J5 combos would tie. Elon is the preflop raiser and plays aggressively postflop, so he’s likely to have Jx, a 5, some weaker pairs or bluffs. I should be extracting value rather than checking and giving a free card. A small-to-medium value bet (~1/2 pot) will keep worse hands (single J, Jx two-pair, stray 5x, draws and weaker pairs) in and can induce raises from JJ or bluffs — and if he raises I’m happy to call (or reraise) given my very strong holding and deep stacks. Bet 300 into the 600 pot to maximize value while keeping him in the hand.

Nut club flush draw (Ac with 5c Jc on board) plus ace-high showdown value on a paired board. Excellent equity (~35-40% against a pair), position, and implied odds with deep stacks. Minni's flop lead likely weak pair (Jx, overpair to 5s), busted AK, or weaker draw; sets unlikely given pre-flop limp-call. Raising builds pot for when we hit flush/straight outs, extracts value from worse, protects vs. gutshots (67 etc.), and has fold equity vs. floats. 3x raise standard size here for balance. Consistent with aggressive image.

I have J♦5♦ on 5♣5♠J♣ — fives full of jacks: a near‑nut hand. The only real hand that currently beats me is pocket jacks (very few combos remain because I hold a Jack), while J5 combos would tie. Elon’s raise to 900 over my 300 on the flop is strong but ambiguous (could be JJ, single Jx, a 5, or a bluff). With deep stacks and such a strong holding I want to build the pot and extract value from worse Jx hands, small pocket pairs and bluffs. A minimum reraise to 1500 (total) charges those hands while keeping them in and still allows me to call any larger action if necessary. Action: raise to 1500 to maximize value and define his range on the turn.

Nut club flush draw with Ac (9 clean outs) plus ace-high showdown value on a paired board. Equity ~35-45% against Minni's likely range of Jx pairs, weaker 5x two-pair, sets (55/J5 rare), or inferior draws (spades, weaker clubs). Her small flop lead followed by 3-bet over my raise smells polarized: value monsters or bluffs/draws trying to take it away given my aggressive image. Pot odds excellent (600 into 3000 for ~14% equity needed, pot becomes 3600). Calling realizes full equity with position (button acts last post-flop), deep implied odds (Minni 14k stack), and avoids overcommitting deep-stacked. Raising risks larger pots OOP if she jams sets/draws; shoving too thin. Consistent aggression but balanced here. History shows Minni calls stations but folds to pressure sometimes; call keeps deception and extracts on brick turns.

Board: 5♣ 5♠ J♣ 9♥; I hold J♦5♦ — fives full of jacks (near‑nut). The only hand that currently beats me is pocket Jacks (very few combos). Elon showed strength with a flop raise and calling my reraise, but his range still includes many worse Jx hands, a remaining 5 (trip), medium pairs and bluffs that will call a sizable turn bet. I should be extracting value rather than checking and letting him realize equity. Betting ~2/3 pot (~2400 into 3600) charges worse Jx/trips and smaller pairs while keeping weaker hands and bluffs in; if he raises big he’s representing one of the very few hands that beat me (or a huge bluff), and I can fold only to extreme action or call/shove depending on size. Action: bet 2400 to maximize value and define his range.

Turn pairs my 9, giving second pair with nut club flush draw (9 outs) and ace kicker for showdown value. Board paired 5s, possible sets (55 likely from limp-call), Jx (JT/JQ), 5x two-pair, or strong draws (KQ clubs, spade draws). Her line: limp-call pre (weak-strong), small flop donk then 3bet raise (polarized to monsters/bluffs), turn bet continues pressure. Equity strong (~45%+ vs range excluding nuts, even 30% vs sets). Pot odds great (2400 into 6000 = 29% needed). Raising builds pot for river value/implied odds (her 9k left), protects vs river gutshots (67, T7), extracts from worse pairs/draws, fold equity vs bluffs/inferior equity. Call passive now with hand strength improved; pot would bloat OOP risk. Shove too thin deep-stacked (20 hands left, preserve fold equity). Minni calls stations but folds flops/turns to pressure historically. Pot-raise to 6000 total sizes well (3x her bet), consistent aggression balances prior calls.

I have J♦5♦ on 5♣5♠J♣9♥ — fives full of jacks: a near‑nut full house. The only realistic hand that currently beats me is pocket jacks (very few combos). Elon’s turn raise is strong but his range still contains many worse Jx, single pairs and bluffs that I want to keep in. Calling 3,600 into a 12,000 pot (~23% immediate pot odds) is an easy value call given my equity. Raising to the min (9,600) or shoving risks folding out a lot of worse hands and over‑commits most of my stack for little extra EV. I’ll call now (total committed this street = 6,000), keep worse hands and bluffs in, and re‑evaluate/respond on the river.

I have J♦5♦ on 5♣5♠J♣9♥6♠ — fives full of jacks: a near‑nut full house. The only realistic hand that beats me is pocket jacks (very few combos). Elon has been aggressive (preflop raise, flop/turn action), so his range contains lots of single Jx, weaker pairs and bluffs that I want to extract from. Checking risks giving up value if he checks back; shoving will fold out many worse hands and overcommit into the one beating combo. I choose a large but not all‑in value bet to get called by worse Jx/top‑pair hands and bluffs while leaving some stack to respond to a raise. Bet 6000 into the 15600 pot — big enough to be called by worse hands, but not so huge that I immediately fold out thin value or have no maneuvering room if Elon raises.

River completes possible spade flushes (e.g., AsXs, 7sTs) and straights (e.g., 78, 47, T7), though less likely given board texture. My Ac9c gives pair of 9s with Ace kicker, beating bluffs, weaker pairs (6x, Tx), and some busted draws, but loses to any Jx (pair of Js), 5x (trips 5s), full houses (J5, 55 quads). Minni's line is extremely strong: limp-called pre (loose/weak-strong), donk-led flop paired/clubby board, 3-bet my raise (polarized nuts/bluff, but sizing suggests value), turn donk bet into raised pot, called my raise (pot-committing draw or monster), river donk-lead 6000 into 15600 pot (~38% pot, value sizing). This reps Jx/5x monsters over bluffs, especially post-spade. Equity <20% vs value-heavy range (e.g., 55/J5/JcX clubs/5x/Jx); pot odds 21600:6000 (~27%) require too much bluff % given history (Minni calls stationy but bets/folds to aggression, rarely bluffs rivers after multi-street value lines). Deep stacks pre-river but ICM/endgame (20 hands left HU) favors folding thin value spots; preserve ~16k stack for better. Consistent with balanced aggression (valued turn pair+draw, fold river brick).

Heads-up on the button with KcTd — a strong, high-card hand that plays well in position. I want to seize initiative and deny the BB profitable, cheap calls, so I’ll open to 3x the big blind (to 300). This sizing folds out weaker hands, isolates Elon, and gives me position postflop to steer the pot. If he calls I’ll C-bet on favorable/dry flops and use pot control on dangerous textures; if he 3-bets large I’ll usually fold KTo (only calling small 3-bets or against a clear, exploitable bluff-heavy 3-bet frequency).

Heads-up pre-flop in BB vs button raise to 300 (3BB). My hand is 42s, a suited connector with straight/flush potential and decent playability. Pot odds are excellent: calling 200 more into 400 pot (~3:1 implied). In heads-up NLHE, BB defends very wide (~50%) vs button opens due to position OOP but great pot odds and ability to realize equity post-flop. Minni is aggressive, raises frequently from history, likely wide range. 42s has solid equity (~35-40% vs typical button open range) and plays well multiway or OOP. Folding trash, but this is playable. No need to 3-bet bluff as blockers are poor. Calling to see flop OOP and play accordingly. Late in tournament (131/150), stacks deep (160BB eff.), variance manageable for max EV.

Heads-up on flop, OOP with 42hh on 5c6sTc board. Complete air: no pair, no flush draw (hearts), no straight draw (gutshot to 3 is unlikely and weak). Pot 600, stacks deep (160BB eff). Minni's button raise range is wide (pairs, broadways, suited connectors, Ax suited/off). This flop hits her range decently (TT+, 65s, T6s, T5s, flush draws like AcXc, KcQc, etc.), but she cbets wide (~60-70%) as aggressor IP. Checking is standard: preserves stack, realizes fold equity by checking (lets her bluff), can fold to cbet cheaply or trap if she checks back. Donk betting air OOP is -EV: risks raise, no fold equity like IP. Calling any bet is disaster (0 equity). Plan: check, fold to most bets unless tiny, evaluate turn if checked to. Late tournament, ICM not extreme yet with deep stacks/2 players left.

Heads-up I opened and Elon called with a very wide range. Flop 5c 6s Tc gives me top pair (T) with the best available kicker (K) and a backdoor club; this is a clear spot to c-bet for value and protection. I want to extract from worse tens, small pairs and charge any club/straight draw hands while denying free cards, so I’ll bet ~2/3 pot. If he calls I can continue based on the turn; if he check-raises huge I’ll strongly consider folding to a line representing two pair/set or a polarized shove.

Heads-up on flop OOP with 42hh on 5c6sTc rainbow-ish board (two clubs). I defended wide preflop from BB vs button raise, correct for position/pot odds. Flop hits villain's range decently (sets, two-pair like T6/T5/65, pairs + draws, flush draws) but she cbets frequently IP (~2/3 pot standard). My hand: gutshot straight draw to 3 (~4 outs, 8%ish equity), backdoor flush/straight. No showdown value. Calling 400 into 1000 (~28% pot odds needed) OOP is -EV long-term: turns bad (positionally weak, villain can repop value/overbet), river equity thin.
